5-amino-1mq.subtitle: NNMT Inhibitor | Longevity & Metabolic Enhancement
5-amino-1mq.descriptionEn: 5-Amino-1MQ is a small molecule NNMT (nicotinamide N-methyltransferase) inhibitor that enhances cellular NAD+ levels and mitochondrial function. Originally developed for metabolic research, it's gaining attention for its potential anti-aging properties and ability to improve energy metabolism at the cellular level.
5-amino-1mq.keyBenefits: Enhanced NAD+ levels, improved mitochondrial function, longevity benefits, better energy metabolism, enhanced cellular repair, improved insulin sensitivity, convenient daily dosing
5-amino-1mq.mechanism: Orally administered 5-Amino-1MQ inhibits NNMT enzyme systemically, preventing NAD+ degradation and maintaining higher cellular NAD+ levels throughout the body, supporting mitochondrial function and metabolic processes
5-amino-1mq.protocolTiming: Take 5-Amino-1MQ with food to improve absorption and reduce potential GI effects. Consistent daily timing helps maintain steady NAD+ levels. Morning or early afternoon dosing is preferred to avoid potential sleep interference.Peptide InteractionsNAD+ Precursors (NMN, NR)SynergisticResveratrolSynergi

adalank.subtitle: N-Acetyl Selank Amidate | Enhanced Tuftsin Analog
adalank.descriptionEn: Adalank (N-Acetyl Selank Amidate) is an enhanced synthetic derivative of Selank peptide, featuring N-terminal acetylation and C-terminal amidation for superior stability and blood-brain barrier penetration. Developed from the immunomodulatory peptide tuftsin, it's researched for anxiety reduction, cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and stress resilience with improved pharmacokinetic properties compared to the parent compound.
adalank.keyBenefits: Potent anxiolytic effects, enhanced cognitive function, stress resilience, improved mood and emotional stability, neuroprotection, and immunomodulatory support. Superior bioavailability and extended half-life compared to parent compound Selank.
adalank.mechanism: Adalank crosses the blood-brain barrier via enhanced lipophilicity from N-acetylation and C-terminal amidation. It modulates GABAergic neurotransmission, upregulates BDNF in the hippocampus, influences serotonin metabolism, provides immunomodulatory effects via tuftsin-derived mechanisms, and reduces stress-related neurotransmitters without benzodiazepine dependency.Molecular InformationWeight792.
adalank.protocolTiming: Injectable Adalank is most effective when administered in the morning to align with natural cortisol rhythms and maximize anxiolytic benefits throughout the day. If taking twice daily, second dose should be early afternoon. Avoid evening administration to prevent potential sleep disruption.Peptide I

anxiety.title: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD)
anxiety.description: Parent compound Selank demonstrated anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines in clinical trials with 62 GAD patients, with 40% showing rapid response within 1-3 days. N-Acetyl modification enhances these effects.

anxiety.title: Stress-Related Anxiety
anxiety.description: Research shows Selank reduces stress-induced anxiety through modulation of stress hormones and neurotransmitter balance, with enhanced stability in N-Acetyl form prolonging anxiolytic benefits.

anxiety.title: Anxiety without Sedation
anxiety.description: Unlike benzodiazepines, provides anxiety relief without sedation, amnesia, tolerance, withdrawal, or dependency - critical advantage for long-term anxiety management.

anxiety.title: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
anxiety.description: Clinical trials with intranasal Selank at 2700 mcg/day for 14 days demonstrated anxiolytic efficacy comparable to benzodiazepines in GAD patients without sedation or dependency.

anxiety.title: Rapid Anxiety Relief
anxiety.description: Research shows 40% of patients experience rapid response with significant symptom reduction within 1-3 days of intranasal administration.

anxiety.title: Sustained Anxiolytic Effects
anxiety.description: Anxiolytic effects last for approximately one week after final administration, suggesting sustained neurochemical modulation.

cognitive.title: Cognitive Enhancement
cognitive.description: Selank demonstrates nootropic and psychostimulant effects alongside anxiolytic properties. BDNF upregulation in hippocampus supports memory, learning, and cognitive function.

cognitive.title: Memory Protection
cognitive.description: Research confirms Selank protects against ethanol-induced memory impairment and prevents attention disturbances through BDNF-mediated neurotrophin mechanisms in hippocampus and prefrontal cortex.

cognitive.title: Attention and Focus
cognitive.description: Clinical studies show improvements in attention, mental clarity, and cognitive performance, particularly when combined with reduced anxiety levels.

cognitive.title: Nootropic Enhancement
cognitive.description: Intranasal administration provides psychostimulant and cognitive-enhancing effects alongside anxiety reduction, improving mental clarity and focus.

cognitive.title: Memory and Attention
cognitive.description: Clinical studies demonstrate improvements in memory, attention, and cognitive performance with intranasal Selank administration.

cognitive.title: Antiasthenic Effects
cognitive.description: Reduces asthenia (weakness/fatigue) and improves mental energy and motivation beyond standard anxiolytics.

neuroprotective.title: BDNF Upregulation
neuroprotective.description: Rapidly elevates Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor expression in hippocampus, supporting neuroplasticity, neuronal survival, and long-term cognitive health.

neuroprotective.title: Anti-inflammatory Neuroprotection
neuroprotective.description: Research demonstrates significant modulation of 34 inflammation-related genes, providing anti-inflammatory neuroprotection and immunomodulatory benefits.

neuroprotective.title: Stress Resilience
neuroprotective.description: Enhances resilience to chronic stress conditions and protects against stress-induced neurological damage through multiple neuroprotective pathways.

mood.title: Mood Enhancement
mood.description: Intranasal administration elevates mood and reduces depressive symptoms through serotonin metabolism modulation and BDNF upregulation.

mood.title: Stress Resilience
mood.description: Enhances resilience to chronic stress and improves quality of life in patients with anxiety-asthenic disorders.

mood.title: Emotional Stability
mood.description: Provides balanced emotional state without sedation, maintaining normal cognitive function and responsiveness.

latestResearch.title: N-Acetyl Selank Amidate: Enhanced Pharmacokinetic Properties
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review indicating N-Acetyl Selank Amidate demonstrates 30-50% higher bioavailability than standard Selank, extended half-life (4-6 hours vs 2-3 hours), improved blood-brain barrier penetration, and increased metabolic stability through terminal modifications.

latestResearch.title: Selank Administration Affects GABAergic Neurotransmission Gene Expression
latestResearch.excerpt: Research demonstrates Selank affects expression of genes involved in GABAergic neurotransmission in cells and animals, providing molecular basis for its anxiolytic effects without benzodiazepine-like dependency.

latestResearch.title: Peptide Selank Enhances Benzodiazepine Efficacy While Reducing Side Effects
latestResearch.excerpt: Study in chronic mild stress model demonstrates Selank combination with diazepam enhances anxiolytic effects while decreasing undesirable side effects including attention impairment, memory deficits, asthenia, and sedation.

latestResearch.title: Selank Protects Against Ethanol-Induced Memory Impairment via BDNF Regulation
latestResearch.excerpt: Research confirms Selank produces cognitive-stimulating effects and prevents ethanol-induced memory and attention disturbances through neurotrophin mechanisms related to BDNF production in hippocampus and prefrontal cortex.
latestResearch.source: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine

adamax.subtitle: Next-Generation Semax Derivative | Nootropic Neuropeptide
adamax.descriptionEn: Adamax is a synthetic nootropic peptide and enhanced derivative of Semax, featuring N-terminal acetylation and C-terminal adamantane modification for superior stability and blood-brain barrier penetration. It's researched for cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, neuroplasticity, and potential therapeutic applications in stroke recovery and mood disorders.
adamax.keyBenefits: Enhanced cognitive function, improved focus and mental clarity, neuroprotection, neuroplasticity support, potential mood enhancement, and stress resilience. Superior bioavailability through direct systemic delivery.
adamax.mechanism: Adamax crosses the blood-brain barrier via enhanced lipophilicity from adamantane modification. It upregulates BDNF and TrkB receptor sensitivity, modulates dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin levels, stabilizes microtubules through ADNP-derived mechanisms, and provides antioxidant and anti-inflammatory neuroprotection.Molecular InformationWeight1,032.24 DaLength9 amino acids (with modificatio
adamax.protocolTiming: Injectable Adamax is most effective when administered in the morning to align with natural circadian rhythms and maximize cognitive benefits throughout the day. Consistent daily timing optimizes neuroplasticity effects. Avoid evening administration if sensitive to stimulatory effects.Peptide Interac

adipotide.subtitle: Prohibitin‑Targeting Peptidomimetic | Experimental Anti‑Obesity
adipotide.descriptionEn: Adipotide (Prohibitin‑TP01) is a chimeric, adipose‑vasculature–targeted peptidomimetic that homes to prohibitin/annexin A2 on white adipose tissue endothelium and delivers a pro‑apoptotic D‑(KLAKLAK)2 motif. In obese primates it produced rapid fat loss with improved insulin resistance, but development halted after an early Phase 1 oncology trial; kidney safety signals (reversible proximal tubule changes) remain a key concern.
adipotide.keyBenefits: Rapid fat‑mass reduction in obese non‑human primates with improved insulin sensitivity; direct WAT vascular targeting.
adipotide.mechanism: CKGGRAKDC homes to prohibitin/annexin A2 on white‑fat endothelium; the linked D‑(KLAKLAK)2 disrupts mitochondrial membranes after internalization, triggering localized endothelial apoptosis and adipocyte loss.
adipotide.protocolTiming: Rotate SC sites to reduce irritation; ensure adequate hydration and frequent renal labs during treatment.Peptide InteractionsSemaglutide (GLP‑1 RA)Monitor CombinationTirzepatide (GLP‑1/GIP RA)Monitor CombinationCagrilintide (Amylin analog)UnknownAOD‑9604UnknownBPC‑157CompatibleCJC‑1295 / IpamorelinU

interactions.name: Tirzepatide (GLP‑1/GIP RA)
interactions.description: Overlapping weight‑loss effects; monitor kidney function, volume status, and electrolytes.

interactions.name: Cagrilintide (Amylin analog)
interactions.description: No clinical data on co‑administration; mechanisms differ (satiety vs. adipose vasculature).

interactions.name: AOD‑9604
interactions.description: No published data on combined use or pharmacodynamic interaction.

interactions.name: BPC‑157
interactions.description: No known direct interaction; distinct mechanisms and targets.

interactions.name: CJC‑1295 / Ipamorelin
interactions.description: Metabolic effects may confound body‑composition endpoints; consider staging or separating protocols.

interactions.name: Melanotan II
interactions.description: Theoretical vascular/pressor effects; if studied together, stagger dosing and monitor BP and hydration.

ahk-cu.subtitle: Hair Growth Copper Peptide | Dermal Papilla Stimulator
ahk-cu.routeDetail: TopicalScalp (thinning areas), face, areas needing regeneration
ahk-cu.descriptionEn: AHK-Cu (Copper Tripeptide-3) is a synthetic copper-binding tripeptide composed of Alanine-Histidine-Lysine complexed with a copper ion (Cu²⁺). While structurally similar to the naturally occurring GHK-Cu (differing only in the first amino acid - alanine vs glycine), AHK-Cu demonstrates targeted activity on dermal papilla cells and hair follicles. Research shows it stimulates hair follicle elongation, promotes dermal papilla cell proliferation, increases VEGF production, and inhibits TGF-β1 - making it particularly promising for hair growth applications. It also supports skin regeneration throu
ahk-cu.keyBenefits: Stimulates hair follicle elongation, promotes dermal papilla cell proliferation, increases VEGF for improved scalp circulation, reduces TGF-β1 (counters DHT effects), anti-apoptotic protection for follicles, collagen synthesis support, convenient topical application
ahk-cu.mechanism: AHK-Cu penetrates the scalp to deliver copper directly to dermal papilla cells and hair follicles. It activates proliferation pathways, elevates the Bcl-2/Bax ratio (anti-apoptotic), increases VEGF production for enhanced blood flow, reduces TGF-β1 secretion, and promotes SOD production to reduce oxidative stress. These combined effects support the anagen (growth) phase and may help revive miniatu
ahk-cu.protocolTiming: Apply to clean, dry scalp or skin. Evening application is preferred to avoid UV exposure which can degrade the peptide. If using other actives, apply AHK-Cu first and allow to absorb before layering.Peptide InteractionsGHK-CuSynergisticMinoxidilSynergisticFinasteride / DutasterideComplementaryCopper

interactions.name: GHK-Cu
interactions.description: Complementary copper peptides targeting different aspects of tissue regeneration. GHK-Cu excels at skin repair while AHK-Cu targets hair follicles. Can be combined in formulations

interactions.name: Minoxidil
interactions.description: Both promote hair growth via different mechanisms - minoxidil via potassium channel opening and vasodilation, AHK-Cu via dermal papilla stimulation and VEGF. Often combined in hair loss protocols

interactions.name: Finasteride / Dutasteride
interactions.description: 5-alpha reductase inhibitors block DHT production while AHK-Cu may help counter DHT effects on follicles. Different mechanisms support combination approach

interactions.name: Copper Supplements
interactions.description: Risk of copper toxicity when combined with additional copper sources. Monitor total copper intake and avoid stacking copper peptides with oral copper supplements

interactions.name: BPC-157
interactions.description: Different tissue repair mechanisms - BPC-157 promotes systemic healing while AHK-Cu targets skin/hair. Safe to use in parallel for comprehensive regeneration support

interactions.name: Vitamin C (L-Ascorbic Acid)
interactions.description: Low pH of ascorbic acid can destabilize copper peptides. Use at different times - Vitamin C in morning, AHK-Cu in evening - to avoid interaction

interactions.name: AHA/BHA Acids
interactions.description: Acidic exfoliants (glycolic, salicylic acid) can destabilize copper peptide complexes and reduce effectiveness. Use at different times or in separate routines

interactions.name: Retinol / Retinoids
interactions.description: Both are potent actives that can cause irritation. Retinoids also lower skin pH. Introduce slowly or use at different times to avoid sensitization

interactions.name: Iron Chelators
interactions.description: May interfere with copper binding and reduce peptide effectiveness. Monitor for reduced efficacy if using iron-chelating compounds

interactions.name: Hyaluronic Acid
interactions.description: Excellent combination - hyaluronic acid provides hydration and supports peptide penetration while AHK-Cu promotes follicle and skin health

hairGrowth.title: Anti-DHT Mechanism
hairGrowth.description: Reduces TGF-β1 secretion and oxidative stress (via SOD), both of which contribute to DHT-induced follicle miniaturization

skinRegeneration.title: Extracellular Matrix Support
skinRegeneration.description: Promotes deposition of collagen and other ECM components for tissue renewal and repair

limitations.title: Limited Injectable Research
limitations.description: Most AHK-Cu research focuses on topical application for hair and skin. Injectable protocols are less established than for GHK-Cu

limitations.title: Mesotherapy Applications
limitations.description: Some practitioners use copper peptides in mesotherapy (microinjections) for scalp and facial rejuvenation, but standardized protocols are lacking

limitations.title: Extrapolated from GHK-Cu Data
limitations.description: Injectable dosing is often extrapolated from GHK-Cu research, which has more established injectable protocols

limitations.title: Digestive Degradation
limitations.description: Peptides are broken down by stomach acid and proteases, preventing intact absorption

limitations.title: No Oral Research
limitations.description: AHK-Cu has not been studied for oral administration; all research uses topical or injectable routes

limitations.title: No Nasal Research
limitations.description: AHK-Cu has not been studied for nasal delivery. Its primary applications (hair growth, skin regeneration) require local application

limitations.title: Local vs Systemic Target
limitations.description: AHK-Cu works locally on dermal papilla cells and fibroblasts - nasal delivery would not effectively target these tissues

latestResearch.title: AHK-Cu for Targeted Hair Follicle Regeneration
latestResearch.excerpt: Research confirms AHK-Cu's targeted effects on dermal papilla cells, distinguishing it from GHK-Cu. Increased VEGF and reduced TGF-β1 support the anagen phase and may help revive miniaturized follicles.

latestResearch.title: Copper Peptides in Anti-DHT Hair Loss Strategies
latestResearch.excerpt: AHK-Cu enhances SOD production, reducing oxidative stress that triggers DHT in scalp follicles. Combined with its anti-apoptotic effects, this positions AHK-Cu as a multi-mechanism hair loss intervention.

latestResearch.title: Enhanced Delivery Systems for Copper Tripeptides
latestResearch.excerpt: Liposomal and ionic liquid microemulsion formulations significantly improve AHK-Cu stability and penetration, addressing the peptide's sensitivity to pH and storage conditions.

latestResearch.title: Comparative Analysis: AHK-Cu vs GHK-Cu Applications
latestResearch.excerpt: Studies clarify the distinct applications - GHK-Cu for broad skin regeneration and anti-aging, AHK-Cu for targeted hair follicle and dermal papilla cell stimulation. The alanine substitution confers different receptor binding properties.

aod-9604.subtitle: Advanced Obesity Drug | Modified hGH Fragment 176-191
aod-9604.descriptionEn: AOD-9604 is a modified 17-amino acid fragment of human growth hormone (hGH 176-191) with an added N-terminal tyrosine. It stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis without the growth-promoting effects of full hGH. Unlike intact growth hormone, AOD-9604 does not increase IGF-1, affect glucose metabolism, or bind to the GH receptor.
aod-9604.keyBenefits: Stimulates fat loss through lipolysis without growth hormone side effects. Does not increase IGF-1, affect glucose metabolism, or cause insulin resistance. Also shows promise for cartilage repair.
aod-9604.mechanism: AOD-9604 stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis through beta-3 adrenergic receptor upregulation, without binding to the GH receptor. This selective action provides fat-burning effects without the hyperglycemia, insulin resistance, or cell proliferation associated with full growth hormone.Molecular InformationWeight1,815.1 DaLength17 amino acidsTypeModified hGH C-terminal fragmentAmino Acid

latestResearch.excerpt: In silico analysis suggested that addition of hGH fragment to doxorubicin-loaded chitosan nanoparticles can enhance doxorubicin binding to multiple breast cancer protein targets. These dual-loaded nanoparticles demonstrated greater anti-proliferative activity against MCF-7 breast cancer cells.

latestResearch.title: 14-kDa hGH Fragment: Potent Inhibitor of Angiogenesis and Tumor Metastasis
latestResearch.excerpt: In vivo, the 14 kDa hGH fragment mitigated tumor growth and metastasis of B16-F10 cells and was associated with significant reduction in tumor angiogenesis. Results suggest hGH fragments can be used as therapeutic molecules to inhibit angiogenesis and cancer progression.

latestResearch.title: Human Sports Drug Testing by Mass Spectrometry
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review covering detection methods for AOD-9604 and other peptidic drugs in sports doping. Growth hormone fragments like AOD-9604 remain targets for anti-doping testing due to their performance-enhancing potential.

latestResearch.title: Osteoarthritis Treatment with Intra-articular AOD-9604
latestResearch.excerpt: Intra-articular injection of AOD-9604 combined with hyaluronic acid showed synergistic effects in rabbit osteoarthritis models. The combination accelerated cartilage regeneration and functional recovery without IGF-1 stimulation.

It has completed multiple Phase 2 clinical trials and holds FDA Orphan Drug status for several indications.

bioglutide.subtitle: NA-931 | Quadruple IGF-1/GLP-1/GIP/Glucagon Agonist
bioglutide.descriptionEn: Bioglutide (NA-931) is a first-in-class oral quadruple receptor agonist targeting IGF-1, GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously. Developed by Biomed Industries, this small molecule derived from a cyclic IGF-1 fragment demonstrates remarkable oral bioavailability and blood-brain barrier penetration. Phase 2 clinical trials showed up to 13.8% body weight reduction in 13 weeks while preserving muscle mass - a significant advantage over existing GLP-1 therapies. Currently in Phase 2b/3 trials for obesity and type 2 diabetes.
bioglutide.keyBenefits: Oral administration (no injections), muscle-sparing weight loss, once-daily dosing with or without food, milder GI side effects than injectable GLP-1s, quadruple receptor activation for comprehensive metabolic effects.
bioglutide.mechanism: NA-931 is a small lipophilic molecule derived from cyclic IGF-1 fragment that crosses the blood-brain barrier. Simultaneously activates IGF-1 (muscle preservation, insulin sensitivity), GLP-1 (appetite suppression, insulin secretion), GIP (glucose metabolism, fat storage), and glucagon (energy expenditure, fat oxidation) receptors.Molecular InformationWeightProprietary (small molecule)LengthSmall
bioglutide.protocolTiming: NA-931 can be taken at any time of day with or without food. Unlike oral semaglutide, no fasting period or absorption enhancer required. Half-life of 16-24 hours supports once-daily dosing.Peptide InteractionsTirzepatideBeing StudiedSemaglutideAvoid CombinationRetatrutideAvoid CombinationCJC-1295Com

metabolic.title: Lipid Metabolism

latestResearch.title: NA-931 Phase 2 Success at EASD 2025
latestResearch.excerpt: Biomed Industries presented breakthrough Phase 2 results showing 13.8% weight loss at 150mg daily with 72% of subjects achieving ≥12% reduction. Notably, no muscle loss was observed - a key differentiator from existing GLP-1 therapies.

latestResearch.title: Combination Trial with Tirzepatide Initiated
latestResearch.excerpt: Phase 2 trial (NCT06732245) evaluating NA-931 combined with Tirzepatide in overweight/obese adults. Exploring potential synergistic effects of oral quad-agonist plus injectable dual-agonist.

latestResearch.title: Phase 3 Development Announced
latestResearch.excerpt: Following successful Phase 2 results, Biomed Industries announced advancement to Phase 3 clinical trials for obesity and type 2 diabetes management. Oral administration without absorption enhancers positions NA-931 as potential alternative to injectable GLP-1s.

latestResearch.title: Quadruple Agonism for Obesity - Review
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review discussing multi-receptor agonist approaches for obesity treatment. NA-931's unique quad-agonist mechanism targeting IGF-1, GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors represents next-generation metabolic therapy.

safetyNotes: Investigational drug - not yet FDA approved

Severe diarrhea causing dehydration

Not FDA approved and prohibited by WADA (added 2022) under S0: Non-Approved Substances - no TUE available for athletes.

interactions.name: Semaglutide

interactions.name: Tirzepatide
interactions.description: No known direct interactions. Both are effective weight loss agents with different mechanisms - cagrilintide targets amylin/calcitonin receptors while tirzepatide targets GLP-1/GIP receptors.

interactions.name: Retatrutide
interactions.description: Both cause significant GI effects (gastroparesis, nausea). Cagrilintide (amylin agonist) and Retatrutide (triple GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agonist) have different mechanisms but compounded GI side effects create substantial risk. Not recommended without specialist supervision.

interactions.name: Liraglutide
interactions.description: Phase 2 trials show similar efficacy profiles with no adverse interactions. Both GLP-1 agonists and amylin analogs work through distinct satiety pathways.

interactions.name: Insulin
interactions.description: Monitor glucose levels closely as cagrilintide affects gastric emptying and may alter insulin absorption timing. May require insulin dose adjustments in diabetic patients.

interactions.name: Metformin
interactions.description: Well-tolerated combination demonstrated in Phase 2/3 trials. No pharmacokinetic interactions observed between cagrilintide and metformin.

interactions.name: SGLT2 Inhibitors
interactions.description: Clinical trials included patients on SGLT2 inhibitors with no safety concerns. Both mechanisms complement each other for diabetes and weight management.

interactions.name: Pramlintide
interactions.description: Both are amylin receptor agonists with overlapping mechanisms. Combination would provide no additional benefit and may increase risk of gastrointestinal side effects.

interactions.name: Oral Contraceptives
interactions.description: Delayed gastric emptying may affect absorption of oral contraceptives. Space administration by 1 hour before cagrilintide injection.

cjc-ipa-protocol.subtitle: CJC-1295 (no DAC) / Ipamorelin ProtocolMod GRF 1-29 + Ipamorelin | Pulsatile Growth Hormone Optimization
cjc-ipa-protocol.descriptionEn: A combination protocol using CJC-1295 without DAC (also known as Mod GRF 1-29) and Ipamorelin through complementary receptor mechanisms. CJC-1295 without DAC has a short half-life of 30 minutes to 2 hours, enabling pulsatile GH release that mimics natural physiology. Ipamorelin provides selective GH release without affecting cortisol, ACTH, or prolactin. Note: This protocol uses the no-DAC version specifically for pulsatile release patterns.
cjc-ipa-protocol.keyBenefits: Dual-pathway GH optimization through complementary mechanisms. CJC-1295 without DAC (Mod GRF 1-29) and Ipamorelin both have short half-lives (30 min-2 hours), producing synergistic pulsatile GH spikes that mimic natural physiology without cortisol effects.
cjc-ipa-protocol.mechanism: CJC-1295 without DAC (Mod GRF 1-29) stimulates GHRH receptors on pituitary somatotrophs, triggering cAMP/PKA-mediated GH synthesis with a short 30-minute to 2-hour half-life. Ipamorelin selectively activates ghrelin receptors (GHSR1a) via Gq/11-linked calcium signaling without affecting ACTH or cortisol pathways. Together, the dual-pathway activation produces amplified, pulsatile GH spikes. Note:
cjc-ipa-protocol.protocolTiming: CJC-1295 without DAC has a short 30-minute to 2-hour half-life, similar to Ipamorelin (~2 hours). This enables pulsatile GH release rather than sustained elevation. Once daily (before bed) is convenient; twice daily (AM + PM) better mimics natural GH pulsatility and allows receptor recovery between

cjc-1295.descriptionEn: CJC-1295 without DAC, also known as Modified GRF 1-29 or Mod GRF 1-29, is a synthetic analog of growth hormone releasing hormone (GHRH). cyclic-glycine-proline.subtitle: cGP | IGF-1 Bioavailability Regulator & Neuroprotective PeptideNasal
cyclic-glycine-proline.descriptionEn: Cyclic glycine-proline (cGP) is a naturally occurring small cyclic dipeptide belonging to the 2,5-diketopiperazine family. It is endogenous to the human body, found in plasma, breast milk, and cerebrospinal fluid. cGP is a metabolite of insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1) and plays a key role in regulating IGF-1 bioavailability by competing with IGF-1 for binding to IGFBP-3. Research suggests cGP has neuroprotective, nootropic, and cardioprotective properties, with clinical trials showing benefits for cognitive function, stroke recovery, and metabolic health.
cyclic-glycine-proline.keyBenefits: Neuroprotection, cognitive enhancement, IGF-1 optimization, cardiovascular support, metabolic health, convenient oral administration, crosses blood-brain barrier
cyclic-glycine-proline.mechanism: cGP competes with IGF-1 for binding to IGFBP-3, thereby regulating the amount of bioavailable IGF-1 in circulation. It normalizes IGF-1 function - promoting activity when insufficient and inhibiting when excessive. Also acts as positive allosteric modulator of AMPA and GABA-A receptors and increases BDNF levels.Molecular InformationWeight154.17 DaLength2 amino acids (cyclic)TypeCyclic dipeptide (2
cyclic-glycine-proline.protocolTiming: cGP is orally bioavailable with effective brain uptake. Take consistently at the same time daily. Can be used long-term as it is an endogenous compound naturally found in the body.Peptide InteractionsIGF-1SynergisticBPC-157CompatibleSemaxCompatibleSelankCompatibleGH SecretagoguesMonitor CombinationH

neurological.title: Cognitive Enhancement
neurological.description: Research shows higher cGP levels correlate with better memory retention in elderly. cGP increases BDNF and acts on AMPA receptors for pro-cognitive effects.

neurological.title: Neuroprotection
neurological.description: Protects neural stem cells from oxidative stress via Akt/MDM2-p53 pathway. Demonstrated efficacy in stroke and hypoxic-ischemic brain injury models.

neurological.title: Alzheimer's Disease
neurological.description: Mouse studies show significant reduction in amyloid plaque load and improved spatial memory. Clinical trials of cGP analogs ongoing for neurodegenerative conditions.

neurological.title: Parkinson's Disease
neurological.description: Clinical study showed increased CSF cGP levels and reduced anxiety/depression scores in PD patients following supplementation.

metabolic.title: IGF-1 Optimization
metabolic.description: Regulates bioavailable IGF-1 by modulating IGFBP-3 binding. The cGP/IGF-1 ratio is a biomarker for metabolic and cognitive health.

metabolic.title: Diabetes Support
metabolic.description: Clinical trial showed improved vascular complications, normalized blood pressure, and better HbA1c% in type-2 diabetes patients.

metabolic.title: Blood Pressure Regulation
metabolic.description: cGP administration has been shown to reduce systolic blood pressure in clinical observations.

recovery.title: Stroke Recovery
recovery.description: Patients with higher cGP/IGF-1 ratios show more favorable clinical outcomes following stroke. Oral cGP improved task learning in stroke rat models.

recovery.title: Healthy Aging
recovery.description: Increasing cGP/IGF-1 ratio with age associated with normal cognition, while decreasing ratio associated with cognitive decline and dementia.

research.title: Intranasal Administration (Research)
research.description: Animal studies have used intranasal delivery (20 mg/kg) showing efficacy in Alzheimer's models. Not a standard human administration route.

research.title: Subcutaneous (Research Only)
research.description: Some preclinical studies use subcutaneous injection. Not necessary for humans given cGP's oral bioavailability.

research.title: Alzheimer's Disease Research
research.description: Intranasal cGP (20 mg/kg) significantly reduced amyloid plaque load and improved memory in APP/PS1 mouse model over 28 days.

research.title: Direct Brain Delivery
research.description: Intranasal route may provide more direct brain access, though oral cGP also effectively crosses the blood-brain barrier.

limitations.title: No Established Topical Use
limitations.description: cGP's therapeutic effects require systemic delivery to reach the brain and modulate IGF-1 function. Topical application is not a viable route.

limitations.title: Oral Route Preferred
limitations.description: Given cGP's excellent oral bioavailability and ability to cross the blood-brain barrier, oral administration is the recommended route.

latestResearch.title: Type-2 Diabetes Vascular Complications Study
latestResearch.excerpt: Exploratory clinical trial showing natural cGP supplementation (20-45 μg daily) improved protective foot sensation, reduced macroalbuminuria, and normalized blood pressure and HbA1c% in diabetic patients over 6 months.

latestResearch.title: Oral Stroke Recovery in Rats
latestResearch.excerpt: Oral administration of cyclic glycyl-proline facilitated task learning and improved functional recovery in a rat stroke model, demonstrating effective oral bioavailability for neurological applications.

latestResearch.title: Oxidative Stress Protection via MDM2-p53 Pathway
latestResearch.excerpt: cGP attenuates hydrogen peroxide-induced cellular damage in human neural stem cells through activation of Akt signaling and MDM2-p53 pathway modulation, providing mechanistic insight into neuroprotection.

dihexa.subtitle: Synaptogenic Peptide | Cognitive Enhancement & Neuroprotection
dihexa.descriptionEn: Dihexa is a synthetic oligopeptide derived from angiotensin IV that potently enhances cognitive function by promoting synaptogenesis. Originally developed at Washington State University for Alzheimer's disease, it's 7 orders of magnitude more potent than BDNF at stimulating new synaptic connections and uniquely crosses the blood-brain barrier.
dihexa.keyBenefits: Dramatic synaptogenesis promotion, cognitive enhancement, memory improvement, neuroprotection, potential neuroregeneration. May help with Alzheimer's, TBI, and age-related cognitive decline.
dihexa.mechanism: Dihexa binds to hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) with high affinity and potentiates its activity at c-Met receptor, activating PI3K/AKT pathways. This promotes formation of new synaptic connections at levels 10 million times more potent than BDNF. Crosses blood-brain barrier effectively via oral or injectable routes.Molecular InformationWeight504.7 DaLengthModified tripeptide with hexanoic acidTypeO

dsip.descriptionEn: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide) is a naturally occurring nonapeptide (Trp-Ala-Gly-Gly-Asp-Ala-Ser-Gly-Glu) discovered in 1974 in rabbit brain. Originally isolated for its sleep-promoting properties, DSIP has since been found to have diverse neuromodulatory effects including stress reduction, pain modulation, and endocrine regulation, though its exact mechanisms and clinical efficacy remain subjects of ongoing research.

epitalon.subtitle: Synthetic Pineal Tetrapeptide | Telomerase Activator & Anti-AgingNasal
epitalon.routeDetail: InjectableSubcutaneous: abdomen, thigh, or upper arm
epitalon.descriptionEn: Epitalon (Epithalon, AEDG) is a synthetic tetrapeptide consisting of four amino acids (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) based on a naturally occurring pineal gland peptide. Developed by Russian scientist Vladimir Khavinson and studied for over 35 years, Epitalon is primarily researched for its ability to activate telomerase, extend telomeres, and potentially slow cellular aging through multiple mechanisms including melatonin regulation and gene expression modulation. IMPORTANT: Current Western dosing protocols may be 50-100x higher than necessary due to translation errors from Russian literature.
epitalon.keyBenefits: Telomerase activation for telomere extension, enhanced melatonin production, improved immune function, potential lifespan extension, and systemic anti-aging effects through cellular rejuvenation. CORRECTED DOSING: Evidence suggests current Western protocols use 50-100x higher doses than original Russian research.

fat-blaster.descriptionEn: Fat Blaster is a lipotropic injection blend combining L-Carnitine, Methionine-Inositol-Choline (MIC), B vitamins (B6, B12), and NADH. This enhanced formulation builds on traditional MIC injections by adding L-Carnitine for fatty acid transport and NADH for cellular energy metabolism. Formulations vary significantly between vendors, with ingredient ratios and concentrations differing. While individual components have documented metabolic roles with varying levels of clinical evidence, no published studies have examined this specific combination for weight loss.
fat-blaster.keyBenefits: Enhanced lipotropic formula combining L-Carnitine for fatty acid transport, MIC for liver support, B vitamins for energy metabolism, and NADH for cellular energy. Components have varying levels of clinical evidence.

MIC components (methionine, inositol, choline) act as methyl donors and lipotropic agents supporting liver fat metabolism. B6 and B12 support protein and energy metabolism. NADH participates in cellular ATP production. Note: Clinical evidence for this specific combination is absent - effects extrapolated from individual component st

follistatin-344.subtitle: Myostatin Inhibitor | TGF-β Antagonist for Muscle Research
follistatin-344.descriptionEn: Follistatin 344 (FS-344) is a naturally occurring glycoprotein that inhibits myostatin and activin A, members of the TGF-β superfamily that suppress muscle growth. The FS-344 gene encodes a 344-amino acid precursor that is cleaved to produce the circulating FS-315 isoform. Gene therapy studies in primates and human clinical trials for muscular dystrophy have demonstrated significant muscle hypertrophy and strength gains. However, injectable peptide use has very limited human data, a short half-life (~90 minutes), and most research involves gene delivery rather than exogenous peptide administra
follistatin-344.keyBenefits: Myostatin and activin A inhibition, potential muscle growth enhancement, studied for muscular dystrophy therapy
follistatin-344.mechanism: Binds and neutralizes myostatin and activin A, preventing their interaction with ActRIIB receptors on muscle cells. This blocks TGF-β signaling that normally suppresses muscle growth, allowing enhanced hypertrophy.Molecular InformationWeight~37,800 Da (FS-315 mature form)Length344 amino acids (precursor); 315 amino acids (circulating form)TypeGlycoprotein with follistatin domainsAmino Acid Sequenc

foxo4-dri.descriptionEn: FOXO4-DRI (FOXO4-D-Retro-Inverso) is a synthetic 46-amino acid peptide designed to selectively eliminate senescent cells -damaged cells that accumulate with age and secrete harmful inflammatory factors (SASP). The peptide uses D-amino acids in reversed sequence, making it resistant to proteolytic degradation while maintaining binding function. FOXO4-DRI works by disrupting the FOXO4-p53 interaction that keeps senescent cells alive, causing p53 nuclear exclusion and redirecting it to mitochondria
foxo4-dri.keyBenefits: Selective senescent cell elimination, tissue homeostasis restoration, potential anti-aging effects
foxo4-dri.mechanism: Disrupts FOXO4-p53 interaction in senescent cells, causing p53 nuclear exclusion. Free p53 translocates to mitochondria, triggering caspase-dependent apoptosis specifically in senescent cells while sparing healthy cells (11.73-fold selectivity demonstrated).Molecular InformationWeight5,358.2 DaLength46 amino acids (D-isoforms)TypeD-Retro-Inverso peptide with cell-penetrating domainAmino Acid Seque
foxo4-dri.protocolTiming: Cellular uptake within 2-4 hours, detectable for 72+ hours. glutathione.subtitle: Master Antioxidant | Tripeptide | GSHNasal
glutathione.descriptionEn: Glutathione (GSH) is a tripeptide composed of glutamate, cysteine, and glycine, often called the body's 'master antioxidant.' Found in virtually every cell, it plays critical roles in neutralizing free radicals, detoxifying harmful substances, recycling other antioxidants (vitamins C and E), and supporting immune function. Glutathione levels decline with age and are depleted by stress, toxins, and certain diseases. Injectable forms bypass poor oral bioavailability to deliver glutathione directly to tissues.
glutathione.keyBenefits: Direct delivery bypasses poor oral bioavailability. Rapidly increases plasma glutathione levels. Supports detoxification, immune function, and antioxidant defense systems.
glutathione.mechanism: Glutathione directly neutralizes reactive oxygen species via its sulfhydryl group, conjugates toxins for excretion (Phase II detoxification), recycles vitamins C and E, and supports optimal lymphocyte function. The tripeptide's unique gamma peptide bond protects it from degradation by most peptidases.
glutathione.protocolTiming: Glutathione can be administered at any time of day. IV infusions are typically given in clinical settings. For home IM/SubQ injections, consistency in timing is more important than specific time of day.How to Administer1Most glutathione injections come pre-mixed - no reconstitution required2If lyoph

antioxidantSupport.title: Master Antioxidant Function
antioxidantSupport.description: Glutathione is the body's primary intracellular antioxidant. Its sulfhydryl group donates electrons to neutralize free radicals and reactive oxygen species before they damage cells.

antioxidantSupport.title: Antioxidant Recycling
antioxidantSupport.description: Regenerates oxidized vitamins C and E back to their active forms, extending their protective capacity through the glutathione-ascorbate cycle.

antioxidantSupport.title: Cellular Redox Balance
antioxidantSupport.description: Maintains optimal GSH/GSSG ratio (typically >100:1 in healthy cells). This ratio is a key indicator of cellular health and oxidative stress status.

detoxification.title: Phase II Liver Detoxification
detoxification.description: Glutathione S-transferase enzymes conjugate GSH to toxins, drugs, and xenobiotics, converting them to water-soluble compounds for excretion via bile and urine.

detoxification.title: Heavy Metal Binding
detoxification.description: Sulfhydryl group binds toxic heavy metals including mercury, cadmium, lead, and arsenic, facilitating their safe elimination from the body.

detoxification.title: Acetaminophen Detoxification
detoxification.description: Detoxifies NAPQI, the hepatotoxic metabolite of acetaminophen. Glutathione depletion from overdose leads to liver failure; NAC restores GSH for treatment.

immuneFunction.title: T-Cell Support
immuneFunction.description: Essential for T-lymphocyte proliferation and function. GSH-deficient T cells cannot properly expand or mount effective immune responses.

immuneFunction.title: NK Cell Activity
immuneFunction.description: Some research shows up to 400% increase in natural killer cell activity with IV glutathione therapy, though results vary between studies.

immuneFunction.title: Macrophage Function
immuneFunction.description: Modulates macrophage activation, cytokine release, and proper redox activity critical for pathogen elimination.

neurologicalHealth.title: Parkinson's Disease Research
neurologicalHealth.description: GSH is depleted 40% in the substantia nigra of PD patients. Clinical trials of IV and intranasal glutathione show potential symptom improvement, though evidence remains preliminary.

neurologicalHealth.title: Neuroprotection
neurologicalHealth.description: Protects neurons from oxidative damage. Low glutathione is associated with neurodegenerative conditions including Alzheimer's disease and mild cognitive impairment.

neurologicalHealth.title: Blood-Brain Barrier
neurologicalHealth.description: Intranasal administration has been shown to increase brain glutathione levels, potentially overcoming the blood-brain barrier limitation of IV delivery.

skinHealth.title: Skin Lightening Effects
skinHealth.description: Inhibits tyrosinase enzyme and shifts melanin production from eumelanin (dark) to pheomelanin (lighter). Evidence from RCTs is limited and effects are modest.

skinHealth.title: Antioxidant Skin Protection
skinHealth.description: Protects skin from UV-induced oxidative damage. May support overall skin health through reduced oxidative stress rather than direct lightening effects.

skinHealth.title: Important Caution
skinHealth.description: IV glutathione for skin lightening carries serious safety concerns including hepatotoxicity and anaphylaxis. Philippine FDA has issued warnings against this use.

oralSupplementation.title: Limited Standard Bioavailability
oralSupplementation.description: Standard oral glutathione is broken down by digestive enzymes. Studies show minimal increase in blood glutathione levels with regular oral supplements.

oralSupplementation.title: Liposomal Forms
oralSupplementation.description: Liposomal glutathione shows improved bioavailability in studies, with measurable increases in blood GSH levels and GSH/GSSG ratio.

oralSupplementation.title: Sublingual Forms
oralSupplementation.description: Sublingual delivery may bypass GI degradation. Some studies suggest superiority over standard oral forms.

neurologicalSupport.title: Brain GSH Delivery
neurologicalSupport.description: Studies confirm intranasal glutathione increases brain GSH levels, overcoming the blood-brain barrier that limits IV delivery to the CNS.

neurologicalSupport.title: Parkinson's Disease
neurologicalSupport.description: Phase IIb trial showed intranasal GSH is safe and may improve motor symptoms in PD patients, though placebo group also improved.

neurologicalSupport.title: Cognitive Support
neurologicalSupport.description: Research into intranasal GSH for cognitive decline and neurodegenerative conditions is ongoing.

skinApplications.title: Skin Lightening
skinApplications.description: Some studies show modest skin lightening effects with topical glutathione, though evidence is limited and effects are subtle.

skinApplications.title: Antioxidant Protection
skinApplications.description: May provide local antioxidant protection against UV-induced oxidative damage when applied topically.

skinApplications.title: Penetration Limitations
skinApplications.description: Standard glutathione has poor skin penetration. Liposomal or specialized formulations may improve delivery.

latestResearch.excerpt: 2024 study using state-of-the-art MR spectroscopy confirmed GSH depletion in the substantia nigra of Parkinson's patients and its correlation with disease severity and cognitive function.

hcg.subtitle: Gonadotropin Hormone | LH Receptor Agonist
hcg.routeDetail: InjectableSubQ: abdominal fat, thigh. IM: deltoid, gluteal (rotate sites)
hcg.descriptionEn: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (HCG) is a glycoprotein hormone naturally produced by the placenta during pregnancy. It consists of two subunits: an alpha subunit identical to other pituitary hormones (LH, FSH, TSH) and a unique beta subunit that confers HCG's specific biological activity. Pharmaceutical HCG is derived from the urine of pregnant women or produced recombinantly. It binds to and activates LH receptors, making it valuable for treating hypogonadism, infertility, and cryptorchidism. In TRT protocols, HCG is commonly used to maintain testicular function, preserve fertility, and prevent
hcg.keyBenefits: Maintains testicular function during TRT, preserves fertility, prevents testicular atrophy, stimulates endogenous testosterone production, induces ovulation in women, treats cryptorchidism in children
hcg.mechanism: HCG binds to LH receptors on Leydig cells in the testes, stimulating testosterone and estrogen biosynthesis. In women, it acts on ovarian theca cells to stimulate progesterone production and triggers final oocyte maturation. Half-life is approximately 24-36 hours with peak levels 6-12 hours post-injection. Bioavailability is 40-50% via subcutaneous or intramuscular routes.Molecular InformationWeig
hcg.protocolTiming: Administer HCG 2-3 times weekly, evenly spaced. For TRT, many inject HCG on days between testosterone injections. Consistency of schedule is more important than specific timing.Peptide InteractionsTestosteroneSynergistic - Common CombinationClomiphene (Clomid)Use SequentiallyAromatase InhibitorsComm

interactions.description: Both stimulate testosterone production via different mechanisms. Sometimes used sequentially in PCT protocols. Generally not combined simultaneously - choose one approach

interactions.name: Aromatase Inhibitors
interactions.description: HCG increases intratesticular aromatase activity and may elevate estrogen. AIs like anastrozole are often used concurrently to manage estrogen levels during TRT+HCG protocols

interactions.name: GnRH Analogs
interactions.description: GnRH agonists/antagonists suppress LH. HCG may be used to maintain testicular function during GnRH analog therapy or for recovery afterward. Timing coordination required

interactions.name: FSH (Follitropin)
interactions.description: Combined HCG + FSH therapy is standard for male infertility treatment in hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, achieving spermatogenesis in 70-90% of patients

interactions.name: Kisspeptin
interactions.description: Both stimulate the HPG axis via different mechanisms. Kisspeptin acts upstream at the hypothalamus while HCG acts directly on gonads. Potentially synergistic for fertility restoration

interactions.name: Thyroid Hormones
interactions.description: High-dose HCG has weak TSH-like activity due to alpha subunit homology. Can cause transient hyperthyroidism with very high HCG levels (pregnancy, tumors). Monitor thyroid function

interactions.name: Metformin
interactions.description: In women with PCOS undergoing ovulation induction, metformin may reduce ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome risk when combined with HCG. Often used together in fertility protocols

approvedUses.title: Cryptorchidism (Undescended Testes)
approvedUses.description: FDA-approved for prepubertal cryptorchidism not due to anatomical obstruction. Dosing: 1000-5000 IU 2-3 times weekly for 3-4 weeks. Success rate ~25% as monotherapy

approvedUses.title: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism (Males)
approvedUses.description: FDA-approved for secondary hypogonadism to stimulate testosterone production. Often combined with FSH for spermatogenesis induction. Dosing: 1000-2000 IU 2-3 times weekly

approvedUses.title: Ovulation Induction (Females)
approvedUses.description: FDA-approved as trigger for final follicular maturation and ovulation in assisted reproduction. Single dose of 5000-10,000 IU (or 250 mcg recombinant) when lead follicle reaches 18-20mm

offLabelResearch.title: TRT Adjunct (Fertility Preservation)
offLabelResearch.description: Commonly used off-label during testosterone replacement to maintain intratesticular testosterone, prevent testicular atrophy, and preserve fertility. Typical dose: 250-500 IU EOD or 500-1000 IU twice weekly

offLabelResearch.title: Post-Cycle Therapy (PCT)
offLabelResearch.description: Used to restore testicular function after anabolic steroid use. Stimulates Leydig cells to resume testosterone production while HPTA recovers

offLabelResearch.title: HCG Monotherapy for Hypogonadism
offLabelResearch.description: Alternative to TRT in younger men or those desiring fertility. Can increase testosterone while maintaining testicular function. Requires higher doses: 1500-3000 IU 2-3 times weekly

bodyComposition.title: Testosterone Support
bodyComposition.description: By maintaining testicular testosterone production, HCG indirectly supports lean mass, fat distribution, and metabolic health during TRT

bodyComposition.title: Testicular Volume Preservation
bodyComposition.description: Prevents the testicular shrinkage that occurs with exogenous testosterone use by maintaining Leydig and Sertoli cell stimulation

bodyComposition.title: Fertility Maintenance
bodyComposition.description: Preserves spermatogenesis potential by maintaining the intratesticular testosterone levels required for sperm production

limitations.title: Complete Protein Degradation
limitations.description: As a large glycoprotein (237 amino acids), HCG is completely broken down by stomach acid and digestive enzymes before any absorption could occur

limitations.title: No Oral Bioavailability
limitations.description: The large molecular size (36.7 kDa) and complex glycoprotein structure prevent any meaningful absorption from the GI tract

limitations.title: 'HCG Diet' Drops Are Not HCG
limitations.description: The FDA has warned that 'homeopathic HCG' and oral HCG diet products are fraudulent. They contain no real hormone and provide no benefit beyond placebo effect with very low calorie diet

limitations.title: FDA Warning
limitations.description: FDA and FTC have taken action against companies selling 'oral HCG' products as weight loss aids, deeming them illegal and fraudulent

limitations.title: Molecular Size Barrier
limitations.description: At 36,700 Da, HCG cannot penetrate the skin barrier. Transdermal delivery is not feasible for such large glycoproteins

limitations.title: Systemic Distribution Required
limitations.description: HCG must reach gonadal tissue via bloodstream to exert its effects. Topical application cannot achieve this

limitations.title: No Research Support
limitations.description: No credible research supports topical HCG delivery. Any 'topical HCG' products are not legitimate therapeutic options

limitations.title: Molecular Size Challenge
limitations.description: HCG is far too large for reliable nasal absorption. The glycoprotein structure adds additional complexity

limitations.title: Dosing Precision Required
limitations.description: HCG therapy requires precise dosing and monitoring. Nasal delivery cannot provide the reliability needed for clinical use

limitations.title: No Approved Products
limitations.description: No nasal HCG formulations have been developed or tested. All legitimate HCG is delivered via injection

latestResearch.title: Low-Dose HCG in TRT: Optimizing Protocols
latestResearch.excerpt: Recent studies support low-dose HCG (500-1000 IU twice weekly or 250 IU daily) during TRT to maintain testicular function, intratesticular testosterone, and fertility potential without excessive estrogen conversion.

latestResearch.title: HCG and Leydig Cell Preservation
latestResearch.excerpt: Research confirms HCG prevents Leydig cell atrophy during exogenous testosterone use. Maintaining intratesticular testosterone with HCG preserves the testicular microenvironment critical for future fertility.

latestResearch.title: Dual Trigger Protocols in IVF
latestResearch.excerpt: Combining GnRH agonist trigger with low-dose HCG (dual trigger) in IVF cycles reduces OHSS risk while maintaining oocyte maturation and luteal support, improving outcomes in high-responder patients.

latestResearch.title: HCG for Hypogonadism: Updated Guidelines
latestResearch.excerpt: American Urological Association guidelines recognize HCG as first-line therapy for hypogonadal men desiring fertility, either as monotherapy or combined with clomiphene or TRT.

hexarelin.subtitle: Hexapeptide GHRP | Growth Hormone Release & CardioprotectionNasal
hexarelin.routeDetail: InjectableSubQ: Lower abdomen (2 inches from navel), outer thigh, back of upper arm
hexarelin.descriptionEn: Hexarelin (also known as Examorelin) is a synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone releasing peptide (GHRP) and one of the most potent GH secretagogues studied. Derived from GHRP-6 by Mediolanum Farmaceutici, it stimulates growth hormone release through the ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) and uniquely binds to cardiac CD36 receptors, providing cardioprotective effects independent of GH release. Hexarelin reached Phase II clinical trials for growth hormone deficiency and congestive heart failure but was discontinued in 2005 for strategic reasons. Research demonstrates its potential in cardioprotection,
hexarelin.keyBenefits: Most potent GHRP for GH release, unique cardioprotective effects via CD36 receptor, supports tissue repair and recovery, neuroprotective properties
hexarelin.mechanism: Binds to ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) on pituitary somatotrophs to stimulate pulsatile GH release. Also activates cardiac CD36 receptors providing GH-independent cardioprotection through anti-apoptotic and anti-fibrotic mechanisms.Molecular InformationWeight887.04 DaLength6 amino acidsTypeSynthetic hexapeptide (GHRP)Amino Acid Sequence:His-D-2-methyl-Trp-Ala-Trp-D-Phe-Lys-NH2* Derived from GHRP-6 wi
hexarelin.protocolTiming: Administer on empty stomach (2+ hours after eating, 30+ minutes before eating) for optimal GH release. Evening dose 30 minutes before bed maximizes nocturnal GH pulse.Peptide InteractionsCJC-1295 (without DAC)SynergisticSermorelinSynergisticIpamorelinCompatibleGHRP-6Use CautionGHRP-2Use CautionCJC-1

interactions.name: CJC-1295 (without DAC)
interactions.description: GHRP + GHRH combination provides complementary GH release through different receptor pathways. Studies show synergistic peak GH secretion rates greater than the arithmetic sum of isolated administration.

interactions.name: Sermorelin
interactions.description: GHRH analog enhances hexarelin's pulsatile GH release. Research demonstrates synergistic effects when co-administered, though synergy diminishes with repeated dosing.

interactions.name: Ipamorelin
interactions.description: Both are GHRPs with different selectivity profiles. Combining may soften hexarelin's cortisol/prolactin elevation while maintaining GH output through complementary receptor activity.

interactions.name: GHRP-6
interactions.description: Both GHRPs stimulate GH through similar pathways. Combination may cause excessive GH spikes and additive side effects including cortisol and prolactin elevation.

interactions.name: GHRP-2
interactions.description: Structurally similar GHRPs with overlapping mechanisms. Combining provides diminishing returns with increased risk of hormonal side effects.

interactions.name: CJC-1295 with DAC
interactions.description: DAC version provides continuous GH elevation which may conflict with hexarelin's pulsatile release pattern, potentially reducing synergistic benefits and accelerating desensitization.

interactions.name: MK-677
interactions.description: Both stimulate GH through ghrelin receptor. MK-677's continuous oral action combined with hexarelin may lead to excessive and prolonged GH elevation requiring monitoring.

interactions.name: Tesamorelin
interactions.description: FDA-approved GHRH analog works through complementary pathway. Studies on GHRH + hexarelin combinations show synergistic GH release significantly greater than either alone.

interactions.name: HGH
interactions.description: Exogenous HGH suppresses natural GH release pathways. Combining defeats hexarelin's purpose and may cause excessive GH levels with increased side effects.

interactions.name: BPC-157
interactions.description: Different mechanisms with no known interactions. BPC-157's tissue repair properties may complement hexarelin's recovery and cardioprotective effects.

hgh.subtitle: Human Growth Hormone | 191aa Recombinant Growth Hormone
hgh.routeDetail: InjectableAbdomen (most common), thigh, upper arm - rotate sites
hgh.descriptionEn: Human Growth Hormone (HGH), also known as somatropin or somatotropin, is a 191 amino acid single-chain polypeptide hormone naturally produced by the anterior pituitary gland. Recombinant HGH (rhGH/somatropin) is bioidentical to endogenous growth hormone and is FDA-approved for growth hormone deficiency in children and adults, as well as several other conditions. The '191aa' designation refers to its complete 191 amino acid structure, distinguishing it from older 192aa formulations. HGH regulates growth, metabolism, body composition, and numerous physiological processes through direct actions a
hgh.keyBenefits: Improved body composition (increased lean mass, decreased fat mass), enhanced bone mineral density, improved lipid profile, increased exercise capacity, better quality of life and mood, skin and hair improvements, enhanced recovery and healing
hgh.mechanism: HGH binds to GH receptors on target tissues, triggering JAK2-STAT5 signaling pathway. Direct effects include lipolysis, protein synthesis stimulation, and metabolic regulation. Indirect effects occur via IGF-1 stimulation in liver and peripheral tissues, promoting growth, cell proliferation, and anabolism. Half-life: ~3 hours subcutaneously; peak levels 3-5 hours post-injection.Molecular Informati
hgh.protocolTiming: Morning fasted injection maximizes fat-burning potential. Evening injection mimics natural nocturnal GH pulse. At doses >4 IU, splitting into AM and PM injections can reduce side effects and improve utilization.Peptide InteractionsInsulinComplex InteractionIGF-1 / IGF-1 LR3Synergistic but CautionThy

approvedUses.title: Pediatric Growth Hormone Deficiency
approvedUses.description: FDA-approved for children with GHD, including idiopathic and organic causes. Also approved for Turner syndrome, Prader-Willi syndrome, SGA, Noonan syndrome, SHOX deficiency, and chronic renal insufficiency

approvedUses.title: Adult Growth Hormone Deficiency

approvedUses.title: HIV-Associated Wasting

offLabelResearch.title: Injury Recovery

bodyComposition.title: Lean Mass Increase

limitations.title: Complete Protein Degradation
limitations.description: As a 191 amino acid protein, HGH is broken down into individual amino acids by stomach acid and digestive enzymes before any absorption

limitations.title: No Oral Bioavailability

limitations.title: 'Oral HGH' Products Are Not HGH

limitations.title: Molecular Size Barrier

limitations.title: Systemic Distribution Required
limitations.description: GH effects require distribution to liver (IGF-1 production), adipose tissue, muscle, bone, and other organs - impossible via topical route

limitations.title: No Research Support
limitations.description: No credible research supports topical HGH delivery for systemic effects. Any topical 'HGH' products are marketing gimmicks

limitations.description: HGH is too large for reliable nasal absorption. Bioavailability would be highly variable and likely insufficient

limitations.title: Dosing Precision Required
limitations.description: HGH therapy requires precise dosing based on IGF-1 response and clinical monitoring - nasal delivery cannot provide this precision

limitations.title: No Approved Products
limitations.description: No nasal HGH formulations have been developed, tested, or approved. All legitimate HGH is delivered via subcutaneous injection

latestResearch.title: Long-Acting Growth Hormone Preparations - FDA Approvals
latestResearch.excerpt: FDA has approved weekly somatropin formulations (Sogroya, Skytrofa) for both pediatric and adult GHD, offering improved convenience over daily injections while maintaining efficacy and safety.

latestResearch.title: 15,809-Patient KIMS Safety Analysis - Final Results
latestResearch.excerpt: Final analysis of the largest GH safety database confirms long-term safety with cancer incidence comparable to general population (SIR 0.92), supporting continued use in adult GHD.

latestResearch.title: GH, IGF-I, and Insulin Interplay Review
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review of how growth hormone, IGF-1, and insulin continuously modify each other's production and actions, with implications for metabolic management during GH therapy.

latestResearch.title: Guidance for Long-Acting GH Treatment in Adult GHD
latestResearch.excerpt: Clinical guidance for somapacitan (long-acting GH) treatment including patient selection, dosing, monitoring, and management of side effects in adult growth hormone deficiency.

igf-1-lr3.subtitle: Modified Growth Factor Analog | Research Chemical OnlyNasal
igf-1-lr3.routeDetail: InjectableSubQ: Lower abdomen, outer thigh, upper arm. Rotate sites daily.
igf-1-lr3.descriptionEn: IGF-1 LR3 is a synthetic 83-amino acid analog of human insulin-like growth factor-1 that has never been approved for human use in any country. Despite approximately 3x greater potency than native IGF-1 with a 20-30 hour half-life due to reduced IGF-binding protein interaction, no human clinical trials have ever been conducted. The compound is prohibited in competitive sports, carries significant risks including severe hypoglycemia and cancer proliferation, and remains strictly a research chemical for laboratory use only.
igf-1-lr3.keyBenefits: Approximately 3x more potent than native IGF-1 with 20-30 hour half-life due to reduced IGFBP binding. Promotes both muscle hypertrophy and hyperplasia in animal models.
igf-1-lr3.mechanism: Full IGF-1 receptor agonist with reduced IGF-binding protein interaction. N-terminal extension and R3 substitution prevent sequestration, maintaining free circulating levels. Activates PI3K/Akt/mTOR and MAPK/ERK pathways.
igf-1-lr3.protocolTiming: Post-workout window critical for safety and efficacy. IGF-1 receptors most sensitive when muscles depleted. Must consume carbohydrates immediately to prevent dangerous hypoglycemia lasting up to 30 hours.Peptide InteractionsHuman Growth HormoneAvoid CombinationCJC-1295/IpamorelinUse CautionMK-677Mon

muscleGrowth.title: Muscle Hypertrophy
muscleGrowth.description: Animal studies show 15-20% lean mass gains in 4 weeks through satellite cell activation and protein synthesis enhancement. No human data exists.

muscleGrowth.title: Anti-Catabolic Effects
muscleGrowth.description: Preserves lean mass in cachexia models through ubiquitin-proteasome inhibition. Cancer cachexia rats maintained 30% more muscle versus placebo.

muscleGrowth.title: Hyperplasia Induction
muscleGrowth.description: Unique ability to create new muscle fibers via satellite cell differentiation, not just enlarging existing fibers. Permanent structural changes possible.

tissueRepair.title: Wound Healing Acceleration
tissueRepair.description: Promotes keratinocyte migration, epithelialization, and collagen synthesis. Mouse studies show faster wound closure and reduced scarring.

tissueRepair.title: Ligament/Tendon Recovery
tissueRepair.description: Rat MCL injury model showed improved maximum force and stress with enhanced type-I collagen expression. May overcome mechanical unloading effects.

tissueRepair.title: Burn Recovery
tissueRepair.description: Children with severe burns treated with native IGF-1 showed protein-sparing effects. LR3 variant untested in humans but theoretically more potent.

metabolic.title: Nutrient Partitioning
metabolic.description: Directs calories toward muscle rather than fat storage. Inhibits glucose uptake in adipocytes while promoting muscle uptake.

metabolic.title: Fat Loss Enhancement
metabolic.description: Promotes lipolysis and fatty acid oxidation independent of caloric deficit. Particularly targets visceral adipose tissue in animal models.

metabolic.title: Insulin Sensitivity (Acute)
metabolic.description: Short-term use may improve glucose uptake, though chronic exposure causes paradoxical insulin resistance through receptor downregulation.

metabolic.title: Systemic Absorption Unknown
metabolic.description: Unclear if intranasal delivery achieves systemic levels similar to injection.

metabolic.title: Hypoglycemia Risk Undefined
metabolic.description: Blood sugar effects from nasal administration not studied.

metabolic.title: Not Recommended
metabolic.description: Injectable route preferred for any metabolic research applications.

neuroprotection.title: Alzheimer's Plaque Modulation
neuroprotection.description: Seven-month mouse study showed plaque remodeling and reduced Aβ oligomers but no cognitive improvement.

neuroprotection.title: Theoretical Neuroprotection
neuroprotection.description: May activate brain IGF-1 receptors for neuronal survival signaling. No human data exists.

neuroprotection.title: Research Tool Only
neuroprotection.description: Used in neurodegenerative disease models. Not established for any therapeutic use.

cognitive.title: No Established Benefits
cognitive.description: Mouse studies failed to show cognitive improvement despite cellular changes.

cognitive.title: Theoretical Mechanism
cognitive.description: IGF-1 receptors present in brain tissue but clinical relevance unknown.

cognitive.title: Research Chemical Status
cognitive.description: Intranasal route remains experimental with no safety data.

latestResearch.title: UK Biobank Cancer Association Study
latestResearch.excerpt: Analysis of 400,000 subjects over 7 years found significant positive associations between elevated IGF-1 levels and multiple cancers including thyroid, colorectal, breast, prostate, and melanoma.

latestResearch.title: Species-Specific Response Variations in IGF-1 LR3
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review reveals contradictory responses across species: robust anabolism in rats, minimal effects in guinea pigs, growth inhibition in pigs, raising concerns about human translation.

latestResearch.title: Black Market IGF-1 LR3 Quality Analysis
latestResearch.excerpt: Anti-doping study found oxidized and degraded forms in confiscated samples. Purity varied from claimed 95-98% with significant contamination concerns.

latestResearch.title: Receptor Desensitization Timeline Study
latestResearch.excerpt: Animal models consistently show IGF-1 receptor desensitization at 6 weeks continuous exposure, necessitating cycling protocols. Recovery requires equal off-period.

ipamorelin.subtitle: Growth Hormone Secretagogue | Selective GHRP
ipamorelin.descriptionEn: Ipamorelin is a selective growth hormone releasing peptide (GHRP) that stimulates natural growth hormone production from the pituitary gland. Known for its clean profile with minimal side effects on cortisol and prolactin levels, making it ideal for body composition, recovery, and anti-aging applications.
ipamorelin.keyBenefits: Optimal growth hormone stimulation with precise dosing and superior bioavailability. Most effective method for body composition, recovery, and anti-aging applications with minimal side effects.
ipamorelin.mechanism: Injectable Ipamorelin provides direct systemic delivery for consistent GH pulse stimulation. Binds selectively to ghrelin receptors in the pituitary to stimulate natural GH release without affecting cortisol, prolactin, or causing significant hunger.Molecular InformationWeight711.85 DaLength5 amino acidsTypeGrowth hormone secretagogueAmino Acid Sequence:Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH₂* Contains non-
ipamorelin.protocolTiming: Injectable Ipamorelin is most effective on an empty stomach. For sleep benefits, inject 30 minutes before bed. For body composition, consider morning fasted injection or pre-workout timing for enhanced fat oxidation.Peptide InteractionsCJC-1295SynergisticBPC-157SynergisticTB-500CompatibleSermorelinS

growthHormone.title: Natural GH Stimulation
growthHormone.description: Stimulates pituitary gland to release growth hormone naturally with consistent elevation 30-60 minutes post-injection, maintaining natural pulsatile patterns.

growthHormone.title: IGF-1 Enhancement
growthHormone.description: Increases insulin-like growth factor-1 production through natural GH pathways, mediating growth hormone's beneficial effects on muscle, bone, and tissue.

growthHormone.title: Anti-Aging Benefits
growthHormone.description: Supports cellular regeneration, collagen synthesis, and metabolic function through enhanced growth hormone pathways without disrupting natural hormone balance.

bodyComposition.title: Lean Muscle Development
bodyComposition.description: Promotes muscle protein synthesis and nitrogen retention when combined with resistance training, leading to increased lean mass over 12-16 weeks.

bodyComposition.title: Fat Loss Support
bodyComposition.description: Enhances lipolysis and metabolic rate through GH-mediated pathways, improving overall body composition and reducing visceral fat.

bodyComposition.title: Bone Density Enhancement
bodyComposition.description: Supports bone formation and mineral density through IGF-1 mediated mechanisms, particularly beneficial for aging populations and athletes.

recovery.title: Sleep Quality Enhancement
recovery.description: Increases slow-wave sleep duration and quality when taken before bed, leading to more restorative sleep and improved morning energy levels.

recovery.title: Exercise Recovery
recovery.description: Accelerates recovery from exercise-induced muscle damage with reduced soreness, faster performance restoration, and enhanced training adaptations.

recovery.title: Tissue Repair and Regeneration
recovery.description: Supports tissue repair and cellular regeneration during sleep through enhanced growth hormone release and improved recovery processes.

notAvailable.title: Gastric Acid Degradation
notAvailable.description: Research shows Ipamorelin is rapidly degraded by stomach acid and pepsin, making oral administration ineffective for growth hormone stimulation.

notAvailable.title: Poor Intestinal Absorption
notAvailable.description: Studies indicate minimal bioavailability when taken orally due to peptide size and charge preventing effective intestinal absorption.

notAvailable.title: No Effective Oral Formulations
notAvailable.description: Despite attempts at enteric coating and absorption enhancers, no oral Ipamorelin formulations have demonstrated clinical effectiveness.

notAvailable.title: Poor Transdermal Penetration
notAvailable.description: Ipamorelin's molecular structure and size prevent effective transdermal absorption, making topical application ineffective for systemic effects.

notAvailable.title: Systemic Target Requirement
notAvailable.description: Growth hormone release requires Ipamorelin to reach pituitary gland receptors through systemic circulation, impossible through topical application.

notAvailable.title: No Clinical Evidence
notAvailable.description: No studies demonstrate any effectiveness of topical Ipamorelin for growth hormone stimulation or any of its associated benefits.

latestResearch.title: Selective Growth Hormone Secretagogue Mechanisms of Ipamorelin
latestResearch.excerpt: Latest research confirms Ipamorelin's unique selectivity for GH stimulation without the cortisol elevation seen with other GHRPs, making it ideal for long-term use.

latestResearch.title: Ipamorelin and Sleep Architecture: Clinical Findings
latestResearch.excerpt: New clinical data shows Ipamorelin significantly enhances slow-wave sleep and REM sleep quality when administered before bedtime.

latestResearch.title: Anti-Aging Applications of Selective GH Stimulation
latestResearch.excerpt: Long-term study demonstrates Ipamorelin's effectiveness in reversing age-related decline in growth hormone without the risks of direct GH administration.

latestResearch.title: Ipamorelin vs Other GHRPs: Comparative Safety Analysis
latestResearch.excerpt: Head-to-head comparison confirms Ipamorelin's superior safety profile with equivalent efficacy to other growth hormone releasing peptides.

kisspeptin.subtitle: KISS1 Gene Product | Reproductive Neuropeptide
kisspeptin.descriptionEn: Kisspeptin is a family of neuropeptides encoded by the KISS1 gene that acts as a master regulator of the reproductive system. It potently stimulates gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) neurons, making it essential for puberty initiation, fertility, and reproductive function. Research explores its therapeutic potential for infertility, low libido, and reproductive disorders.
kisspeptin.keyBenefits: Potent stimulation of reproductive hormones (LH/FSH), restoration of natural testosterone production, fertility enhancement, and sexual function support. Suitable for addressing hypogonadism and reproductive disorders.
kisspeptin.mechanism: Kisspeptin binds to GPR54/KISS1R receptors on GnRH neurons in the hypothalamus, triggering pulsatile release of GnRH. This stimulates pituitary secretion of LH and FSH, which in turn promote gonadal steroid production and reproductive function.Molecular InformationWeight1,213.42 Da (KP-10) / 6,087 Da (KP-54)Length10-54 amino acidsTypeNeuropeptide family (RFamide)Amino Acid Sequence:Kisspeptin-10:
kisspeptin.protocolTiming: Kisspeptin is best administered in the morning to align with natural circadian patterns of reproductive hormones. For fertility applications, timing should be coordinated with menstrual cycle phase under medical supervision. Avoid daily dosing to prevent receptor desensitization.Peptide Interactions
