reproductive.title: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
reproductive.description: Research demonstrates kisspeptin can restore reproductive hormone secretion in patients with functional or congenital hypogonadotropic hypogonadism by stimulating the dormant GnRH axis.

reproductive.title: IVF Ovulation Trigger
reproductive.description: Kisspeptin-54 successfully triggers oocyte maturation in IVF with 45% live birth rate and zero cases of severe OHSS, offering safer alternative to hCG in high-risk patients.

reproductive.title: Hypothalamic Amenorrhea
reproductive.description: Clinical trials show kisspeptin can restore reproductive hormone pulsatility in women with hypothalamic amenorrhea when administered appropriately.

sexual.title: Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (Men)
sexual.description: RCT demonstrated kisspeptin significantly modulates sexual brain processing, increases penile tumescence by 56%, and enhances behavioral measures of sexual desire in men with HSDD.

sexual.title: Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (Women)
sexual.description: Clinical trial showed kisspeptin modulates sexual and attraction brain processing in women with HSDD, with increased self-reported feelings of sexiness vs placebo.

sexual.title: Libido Enhancement
sexual.description: Brain imaging studies reveal kisspeptin deactivates self-monitoring regions while activating sexual arousal centers, potentially enhancing sexual response.

metabolic.title: Energy Metabolism
metabolic.description: Preclinical research suggests kisspeptin signaling influences energy expenditure, locomotor activity, and glucose metabolism, though human applications remain investigational.

metabolic.title: Body Composition
metabolic.description: Animal studies show kisspeptin receptor signaling affects adiposity and body weight regulation, particularly in females, via non-reproductive pathways.

metabolic.title: Glucose Homeostasis
metabolic.description: Research indicates kisspeptin signaling may influence glucose tolerance and insulin sensitivity, though clinical implications require further study.

latestResearch.title: Emerging Therapeutic Potential of Kisspeptin and Neurokinin B
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review of kisspeptin's role in regulating the reproductive endocrine axis and emerging therapeutic applications including IVF ovulation triggering, treatment of reproductive disorders, and potential for sexual dysfunction.

latestResearch.title: Intranasal kisspeptin administration rapidly stimulates gonadotropin release in humans
latestResearch.excerpt: First demonstration that intranasal kisspeptin-54 effectively stimulates LH and FSH release in healthy adults and hypothalamic amenorrhea patients, establishing non-invasive delivery route with stable formulation.

latestResearch.title: Kisspeptins Regulating Fertility: Potential Future Therapeutic Approach in Infertility Treatment
latestResearch.excerpt: Systematic analysis of kisspeptin's therapeutic potential in infertility, reviewing clinical trials and future directions for reproductive medicine applications including PCOS, hypothalamic amenorrhea, and IVF protocols.

latestResearch.title: Kisspeptin and its Current Clinical Status - A Systematic Review
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive systematic review of 29 interventional clinical trials with kisspeptin, establishing its current clinical status and safety profile as multipurpose therapeutic with minimal side effects.

klow.subtitle: ProtocolFour-Peptide Regenerative Blend | GHK-Cu + TB-500 + BPC-157 + KPV
klow.routeDetail: InjectableSubcutaneous: Rotate between abdomen, thighs, upper arms
klow.descriptionEn: KLOW is a research peptide combination that builds upon the GLOW protocol by adding KPV for enhanced anti-inflammatory effects. This 80mg blend typically contains 50mg GHK-Cu, 10mg TB-500, 10mg BPC-157, and 10mg KPV in a 5:1:1:1 ratio. While individual components have varying levels of research support, no clinical trials have examined this specific four-peptide combination. The protocol remains investigational with theoretical benefits based on complementary mechanisms.
klow.keyBenefits: Theoretical synergistic effects from four peptides - tissue repair, anti-inflammation, cellular regeneration
klow.mechanism: Each peptide contributes different mechanisms: GHK-Cu modulates genes, TB-500 enhances cell migration, BPC-157 upregulates growth factors, KPV inhibits NF-κB

This small but powerful peptide exhibits remarkable anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties without the pigmentation effects of full α-MSH. KPV has shown particular promise for inflammatory bowel diseases, skin conditions, and immune modulation through its ability to penetrate cells and interact directly with inflammatory signaling pathways.

l-carnitine.subtitle: Amino Acid Derivative | Energy & Fat Metabolism
l-carnitine.descriptionEn: L-Carnitine (levocarnitine) is a naturally occurring amino acid derivative essential for transporting long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for energy production via beta-oxidation. Synthesized endogenously from lysine and methionine, it plays critical roles in energy metabolism, particularly in high-energy tissues like heart, brain, and skeletal muscle. FDA approved as Carnitor® for treating primary and secondary carnitine deficiency. Various forms exist including Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR)
l-carnitine.keyBenefits: Maximum bioavailability (100% vs 14-18% oral), rapid onset of action, bypasses gut microbiome conversion to TMAO, particularly useful for carnitine deficiency states and clinical settings.
l-carnitine.mechanism: Injectable L-Carnitine delivers the compound directly to circulation, bypassing the limitations of intestinal absorption. This provides immediate availability for fatty acid transport into mitochondria. IV/IM routes are preferred in clinical settings for carnitine deficiency and dialysis patients.
l-carnitine.protocolTiming: Injectable L-Carnitine can be administered any time. For exercise performance, some protocols administer 1-2 hours before training. metabolic.title: Carnitine Deficiency
metabolic.description: FDA-approved indication for primary and secondary carnitine deficiency. Injectable form provides 100% bioavailability for rapid repletion of carnitine stores.

metabolic.title: Dialysis Support
metabolic.description: FDA-approved for end-stage renal disease patients on hemodialysis who lose carnitine during dialysis sessions. Standard protocol is 10-20 mg/kg post-dialysis.

metabolic.title: Fat Metabolism
metabolic.description: Direct delivery supports enhanced fatty acid oxidation and energy production, particularly beneficial when combined with exercise protocols.

cardiovascular.title: Acute Cardiac Events
cardiovascular.description: Meta-analysis of 13 trials showed 27% mortality reduction and 65% reduction in ventricular arrhythmias when used in acute myocardial infarction settings.

cardiovascular.title: Heart Failure Support
cardiovascular.description: Studies show improvements in left ventricular ejection fraction, stroke volume, and cardiac output in chronic heart failure patients.

cardiovascular.title: Peripheral Arterial Disease
cardiovascular.description: Propionyl-L-Carnitine form studied for improving walking distance and blood flow in peripheral vascular disease.

cardiovascular.title: Lipid Profile
cardiovascular.description: Umbrella meta-analysis showed significant reductions in total cholesterol, triglycerides, LDL while increasing HDL cholesterol levels.

cardiovascular.title: Heart Health
cardiovascular.description: Studies in heart failure patients show improvements in ejection fraction and cardiac output. Use in acute MI associated with reduced mortality in meta-analyses.

cardiovascular.title: Blood Flow
cardiovascular.description: Propionyl-L-Carnitine specifically studied for peripheral arterial disease with improvements in walking distance and circulation.

recovery.title: Exercise Recovery
recovery.description: Injectable L-Carnitine studied for reducing muscle damage markers (creatine kinase, myoglobin) and accelerating recovery post-exercise.

recovery.title: Critical Illness
recovery.description: Used in ICU settings at higher doses (up to 300 mg/kg/day) for metabolic support in critically ill patients with carnitine depletion.

recovery.title: Surgical Recovery
recovery.description: Research supports use peri-operatively for cardiac surgery patients to support myocardial energy metabolism.

recovery.title: Exercise Recovery (LCLT)
recovery.description: L-Carnitine L-Tartrate (2g/day) shown to reduce muscle damage markers, improve perceived recovery, and decrease muscle soreness post-exercise in RCTs.

recovery.title: Muscle Damage Reduction
recovery.description: Meta-analysis showed L-Carnitine reduced muscle soreness at 24h and 48h, lowered myoglobin and creatine kinase markers after exercise.

recovery.title: Hormonal Response
recovery.description: LCLT supplementation shown to increase IGFBP-3 and may support androgen receptor density, potentially enhancing recovery hormonal milieu.

weightLoss.title: Body Weight Reduction
weightLoss.description: Meta-analysis of 37 RCTs showed significant weight loss (−1.21 kg), BMI reduction (−0.24 kg/m²), and fat mass decrease (−2.08 kg). Effects most pronounced in overweight/obese individuals.

weightLoss.title: Fat Metabolism
weightLoss.description: Enhances fatty acid transport into mitochondria for oxidation. 2000 mg/day identified as optimal dose for maximum weight management effects.

weightLoss.title: Metabolic Support
weightLoss.description: Supports overall metabolic function by improving the efficiency of fat utilization for energy, particularly during exercise.

cognitive.title: Cognitive Function (ALCAR)
cognitive.description: Acetyl-L-Carnitine meta-analysis showed benefits in mild cognitive impairment and early Alzheimer's disease. Crosses blood-brain barrier to support acetylcholine synthesis.

cognitive.title: Neuroprotection
cognitive.description: ALCAR demonstrates neuroprotective effects via antioxidant mechanisms, mitochondrial support, and enhancement of nerve growth factor activity.

cognitive.title: Mental Energy & Focus
cognitive.description: ALCAR may improve mental energy and clarity by enhancing brain mitochondrial function and neurotransmitter metabolism.

hormonal.title: Male Fertility
hormonal.description: Meta-analyses show L-Carnitine significantly improves sperm concentration, motility, morphology, and testosterone levels in men with idiopathic infertility.

hormonal.title: Testosterone Support
hormonal.description: Studies show increased serum testosterone levels and androgen receptor expression, particularly relevant for men with suboptimal levels.

hormonal.title: Reproductive Health
hormonal.description: Antioxidant effects protect sperm from oxidative damage, improving overall male reproductive parameters.

cellular.title: Mitochondrial Function
cellular.description: Core mechanism involves supporting mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation, the primary pathway for cellular energy production in many tissues.

cellular.title: Chronic Fatigue
cellular.description: Studies in chronic fatigue syndrome show improvements in multiple clinical parameters, potentially through mitochondrial support mechanisms.

cellular.title: Energy Production
cellular.description: Essential for efficient energy production from fats, particularly important in high-energy demand tissues like heart and muscle.

latestResearch.title: Role of L-carnitine in exercise training: Anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and metabolic interactions
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive 2025 review examining L-Carnitine's mechanisms including fatty acid transport, antioxidant effects via SOD, GPx, and catalase upregulation, and anti-inflammatory properties suppressing TNF-α and IL-6.

latestResearch.title: L-Carnitine in the Treatment of Psychiatric and Neurological Manifestations: A Systematic Review
latestResearch.excerpt: 2024 systematic review analyzing L-Carnitine and ALCAR effects across psychiatric and neurological conditions, noting varying effects and the need for condition-specific dosing protocols.

latestResearch.title: The Effects of L-Carnitine Supplementation on Weight Loss, Glycemic Control, and Cardiovascular Risk Factors in Type 2 Diabetes
latestResearch.excerpt: 2024 dose-response meta-analysis of 21 RCTs with 2,041 patients showing every 1g/day L-Carnitine supplementation significantly reduced BMI in type 2 diabetes patients.

latestResearch.title: Revisiting the Role of Carnitine in Heart Disease Through the Lens of the Gut Microbiota
latestResearch.excerpt: 2024 review examining the complex relationship between L-Carnitine, gut microbiome, TMAO production, and cardiovascular health, highlighting individual variability in TMAO conversion.

lipo-c.subtitle: Lipotropic Injection | MIC + B Vitamins
lipo-c.routeDetail: InjectableIM: deltoid, thigh, gluteal | SubQ: abdomen, thigh
lipo-c.descriptionEn: Lipo-C is a lipotropic injection containing Methionine, Inositol, Choline (MIC), and B vitamins that support fat metabolism and liver function. These nutrients act as methyl donors and lipotropic agents, assisting in the breakdown and transport of fats. While individual components have documented metabolic roles, clinical evidence for the combined injection specifically for weight loss remains limited.
lipo-c.keyBenefits: Lipotropic nutrients (methionine, inositol, choline) support liver function and fat metabolism. B vitamins contribute to energy metabolism. Bypasses digestive absorption for direct nutrient delivery.
lipo-c.mechanism: MIC components act as methyl donors supporting Phase II liver detoxification and lipid transport. Choline prevents hepatic fat accumulation, methionine provides sulfur for glutathione synthesis, and inositol aids cellular signaling. B12 supports methylation pathways. Note: Clinical evidence for weight loss specifically from MIC injections is limited.
lipo-c.protocolTiming: Morning injections may be preferred if seeking energy benefits from B vitamins. Timing is flexible - consistency matters more than specific time of day.Peptide InteractionsSemaglutideCompatibleTirzepatideCompatibleB12 SupplementsMonitor CombinationMetforminCompatibleDiabetes MedicationsMonitor Combi

metabolicHealth.title: Methylation Support

metabolicHealth.title: Insulin Sensitivity
metabolicHealth.description: Inositol component has moderate evidence for improving insulin sensitivity, particularly in PCOS populations, with documented reductions in fasting insulin.

fatLoss.title: Fat Metabolism Support
fatLoss.description: Lipotropic agents assist in breakdown and transport of fats. However, direct clinical evidence for MIC injections causing weight loss is limited - benefits likely modest.

fatLoss.title: L-Carnitine Effects

fatLoss.title: Metabolic Rate
fatLoss.description: B vitamins support energy metabolism, though supplementation above adequate levels does not enhance metabolism in non-deficient individuals.

energyMetabolism.title: B12 for Energy
energyMetabolism.description: B12 injections may improve energy in deficient individuals. No evidence that B12 above recommended amounts enhances energy or performance in those with adequate levels.

energyMetabolism.title: Nutrient Delivery
energyMetabolism.description: Injectable delivery bypasses GI absorption, beneficial for individuals with malabsorption issues (gastric bypass, Crohn's, pernicious anemia).

energyMetabolism.title: Fatigue Reduction
energyMetabolism.description: May help reduce fatigue related to nutritional deficiencies. Effects on general fatigue in well-nourished individuals not established.

latestResearch.title: Inositol in PCOS: International Guidelines Update

latestResearch.title: L-Carnitine Supplementation: Updated Meta-Analysis
latestResearch.excerpt: Analysis of 43 RCTs confirms modest weight loss benefits of L-carnitine (-1.21 kg average) with effects most pronounced in overweight/obese individuals combining supplementation with exercise programs.

latestResearch.title: B12 and Lipid Metabolism Review
latestResearch.excerpt: Review examining vitamin B12's role in fat metabolism found deficiencies associated with obesity and metabolic syndrome, though supplementation above recommended amounts shows no weight loss benefit in non-deficient individuals.

ll-37.descriptionEn: LL-37 is the only human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide, a 37-amino acid cationic peptide derived from hCAP18 that exhibits broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi while modulating immune responses and promoting wound healing.

mazdutide.subtitle: Dual GLP-1/Glucagon Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes
mazdutide.routeDetail: InjectableAbdomen, thigh, or upper arm - rotate sites
mazdutide.descriptionEn: Mazdutide (IBI362/LY3305677) is a first-in-class dual glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) and glucagon receptor agonist, and a synthetic analog of oxyntomodulin. Unlike tirzepatide which targets GIP/GLP-1, mazdutide uniquely combines GLP-1's appetite suppression with glucagon's ability to stimulate thermogenesis and increase energy expenditure. Phase 3 trials (GLORY-1, GLORY-2) have demonstrated up to 20% weight loss, with recent head-to-head trials showing superiority over semaglutide for both glycemic control and weight reduction in patients with type 2 diabetes and obesity.
mazdutide.keyBenefits: Up to 20% body weight loss, superior glycemic control vs semaglutide, increased energy expenditure via glucagon receptor activation, improved cardiometabolic markers (BP, lipids, liver fat), once-weekly convenience
mazdutide.mechanism: Dual agonist of GLP-1 and glucagon receptors. GLP-1 activation: stimulates insulin release, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying, reduces appetite via hypothalamic signaling. Glucagon activation: increases energy expenditure and thermogenesis, improves hepatic fat metabolism. The dual mechanism provides synergistic weight loss effects while GLP-1 counteracts glucagon's glucose-raising effec
mazdutide.protocolTiming: Inject at any time of day, with or without food. Choose a consistent day each week. If a dose is missed, take as soon as possible within 3 days, then resume regular schedule.Peptide InteractionsSemaglutideAvoid CombinationTirzepatideAvoid CombinationLiraglutideAvoid CombinationInsulinMonitor Closely

limitations.description: At 4,563 Da, mazdutide far exceeds the ~500 Da limit for effective transdermal absorption

limitations.title: Systemic Action Required
limitations.description: GLP-1 and glucagon receptor effects require systemic delivery to pancreas, hypothalamus, liver, and adipose tissue

limitations.title: No Topical Development
limitations.description: No research into topical delivery systems for mazdutide - subcutaneous injection is the established route

limitations.title: No Research Support
limitations.description: No studies have investigated nasal delivery of mazdutide - all clinical data is from subcutaneous injection

limitations.title: Dosing Precision
limitations.description: The dose titration protocols require precise dosing that nasal delivery cannot reliably provide

limitations.title: Molecular Characteristics
limitations.description: The fatty acid conjugation that enables once-weekly dosing may hinder nasal absorption

latestResearch.title: GLORY-2: Mazdutide 9mg Achieves 20.1% Weight Loss in Phase 3 Trial

latestResearch.title: DREAMS-3: Mazdutide Superior to Semaglutide in Head-to-Head Trial
latestResearch.excerpt: First direct comparison showing mazdutide outperforms semaglutide for both glycemic control (HbA1c <7%) and weight loss (≥10%) in patients with T2D and obesity - 48% vs 21% achieving dual endpoints.

latestResearch.title: China NMPA Approves Mazdutide for Weight Management
latestResearch.excerpt: Mazdutide (Xinermei®) becomes world's first dual GCG/GLP-1 receptor agonist approved for chronic weight management, marking new era in obesity treatment with dual-mechanism approach.

latestResearch.title: GLORY-1 Published in New England Journal of Medicine
latestResearch.excerpt: Landmark Phase 3 trial results published in NEJM demonstrating clinically relevant weight reductions with once-weekly mazdutide 4mg and 6mg over 48 weeks in Chinese adults with obesity.

safetyNotes: Start with lowest dose and escalate gradually every 4 weeks to minimize GI side effects
GI symptoms (nausea, diarrhea, vomiting) are most common - typically mild-moderate and improve over time
Increased heart rate (5-17 bpm) observed but not associated with cardiac events in trials
Monitor for signs of pancreatitis - severe persistent abdominal pain radiating to back
Caution with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome (class warning for GLP-1 agonists)
If taking insulin or sulfonylureas, monitor blood glucose closely and reduce doses as needed
Not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding - insufficient safety data
Stay well hydrated, especially during initial weeks when GI symptoms may occur
stopSigns: Severe or persistent abdominal pain (potential pancreatitis)
Neck lumps, hoarseness, or difficulty swallowing (thyroid concerns)
Severe nausea/vomiting preventing adequate nutrition or hydration
Signs of severe hypoglycemia if diabetic (confusion, sweating, shakiness)
Severe allergic reactions (rash, difficulty breathing, facial swelling)
Persistent diarrhea leading to dehydration
Unusual changes in mood, depression, or suicidal thoughts
Kidney problems (decreased urination, swelling, persistent fatigue)
Signs of gallbladder problems (severe upper right abdominal pain)

melanotan-1.routeDetail: InjectableSubQ: abdomen, thigh, upper arm (rotate sites)
melanotan-1.descriptionEn: Melanotan I (afamelanotide) is a synthetic analog of α-melanocyte stimulating hormone that selectively targets MC1 receptors to stimulate melanin production. While an FDA-approved implant version (SCENESSE®) exists for rare medical conditions, research-grade injectable Melanotan I is studied for its photoprotective and tanning effects through regular subcutaneous administration.
melanotan-1.keyBenefits: Research-grade injectable Melanotan I provides precise dosing control and rapid onset of tanning effects. Unlike the FDA-approved SCENESSE® implant, injectable form allows flexible dosing and can be administered at home. Produces visible tanning within 1-2 weeks with proper UV exposure.
melanotan-1.mechanism: Subcutaneous injection delivers Melanotan I directly into systemic circulation with 100% bioavailability. 30-minute half-life requires multiple daily injections for sustained MC1R activation and melanin production.
melanotan-1.protocolTiming: Multiple daily injections needed due to 30-minute half-life. melanotan-ii.descriptionEn: Melanotan II is a synthetic analog of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH) that stimulates melanin production for skin tanning and has additional effects on sexual arousal and appetite suppression. Originally developed at the University of Arizona as a sunless tanning agent, it has gained attention for its dual effects on pigmentation and sexual function through melanocortin receptor activation.

mk-677.routeDetail: OralOral capsule/solution with water on empty stomach
mk-677.descriptionEn: MK-677 (Ibutamoren) is a selective ghrelin receptor agonist that stimulates growth hormone release without suppressing natural production. This orally active compound increases IGF-1 levels, improves sleep quality, and supports body composition changes through sustained growth hormone elevation.
mk-677.keyBenefits: 97% increase in 24-hour growth hormone secretion, 40-72% IGF-1 elevation, improved sleep quality, enhanced body composition, and sustained effects without suppressing natural GH production.
mk-677.mechanism: MK-677 mimics endogenous ghrelin by binding to GHS-R1a receptors in the hypothalamus and pituitary, triggering pulsatile growth hormone release while maintaining natural circadian patterns. Superior >60% oral bioavailability eliminates injection requirements with 24-hour half-life enabling once-daily dosing.
mk-677.protocolTiming: Take MK-677 at bedtime (around 10:40 PM) on empty stomach to align with natural growth hormone patterns and minimize daytime appetite stimulation. mots-c.routeDetail: InjectableSubcutaneous: abdomen, thigh, upper arm (rotate sites)
mots-c.descriptionEn: MOTS-c is a 16-amino acid mitochondrial-derived peptide encoded by the mitochondrial genome. It acts as a mitohormone that regulates metabolic homeostasis, enhances insulin sensitivity, and promotes longevity. MOTS-c operates through the Folate-AICAR-AMPK pathway and can translocate to the nucleus under metabolic stress to regulate gene expression.
mots-c.keyBenefits: Enhanced insulin sensitivity, improved glucose metabolism, activation of AMPK pathway, mitochondrial function optimization, exercise performance enhancement, and potential longevity benefits.
mots-c.mechanism: MOTS-c operates through the Folate-AICAR-AMPK pathway, inhibiting the folate cycle and leading to AMPK activation. Under metabolic stress, it translocates to the nucleus and binds to stress-response transcription factors (NRF2, ATF1/ATF7) to regulate gene expression involved in metabolism, antioxidant response, and cellular stress adaptation.Molecular InformationWeight2,174.6 DaLength16 amino acid
mots-c.protocolTiming: Optimal timing is morning before exercise. Exercise enhances endogenous MOTS-c production by 11.9-fold in muscle tissue. omberacetam.subtitle: Synthetic Dipeptide | Cognitive Enhancement NootropicNasal
omberacetam.routeDetail: OralOral tablet/capsule or sublingual powder; pair with choline source
omberacetam.descriptionEn: Omberacetam (also known as Noopept or GVS-111) is a synthetic nootropic dipeptide (N-phenylacetyl-L-prolylglycine ethyl ester) developed in Russia in 1996 for its potent cognitive-enhancing and neuroprotective properties. While structurally distinct from racetams, it's considered part of the nootropic family and functions as a prodrug that converts to the active metabolite cycloprolylglycine (CPG). Research indicates it may enhance memory, learning, and neuroplasticity through BDNF/NGF upregulation and glutamatergic modulation at doses 1000x lower than piracetam.
omberacetam.keyBenefits: Primary administration route, rapid oral bioavailability, convenient dosing, well-studied in clinical trials, peak absorption within 7 minutes
omberacetam.mechanism: Rapidly absorbed orally and converted to active metabolite cycloprolylglycine (CPG) which modulates AMPA/NMDA glutamate receptors and enhances cholinergic neurotransmission

orforglipron.subtitle: Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes
orforglipron.descriptionEn: Orforglipron (LY-3502970) is an oral, non-peptide GLP-1 receptor agonist - the first small-molecule GLP-1 to complete Phase 3 trials. Unlike injectable GLP-1s, it requires no injections, refrigeration, or food/water restrictions. Clinical trials show substantial weight loss (up to 12.4% at 72 weeks) and robust glycemic control (HbA1c reductions of 1.3-1.6%). Developed by Chugai Pharmaceutical and licensed to Eli Lilly, FDA approval expected 2026.
orforglipron.keyBenefits: Significant weight loss (up to 12.4% at 72 weeks), robust diabetes control (HbA1c reduction 1.3-1.6%), once-daily oral tablet, no refrigeration or food restrictions, reduced cardiovascular risk markers, preserved muscle mass during weight loss
orforglipron.mechanism: Small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist with biased signaling - preferentially activates G protein/cAMP pathways (enhancing insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon, delaying gastric emptying, reducing appetite) while minimizing receptor desensitization. 79.1% oral bioavailability with 29-49 hour half-life supporting once-daily dosing.
orforglipron.protocolTiming: Orforglipron can be taken at any time of day, with or without food or water. Choose a consistent time daily for optimal compliance. No fasting period or timing restrictions required.Peptide InteractionsSemaglutideAvoid CombinationTirzepatideAvoid CombinationLiraglutideAvoid CombinationInsulinMonitor

weightLoss.title: Obesity Management Without Diabetes
weightLoss.description: FDA-approved based on ATTAIN-1 trial: 17.2mg dose achieved 11.1% weight loss (24.9 lbs) at 72 weeks in non-diabetic adults with obesity. 9mg achieved 8.3% (18.9 lbs), 5.5mg achieved 7.4% (17.2 lbs). First oral small-molecule GLP-1 approved for weight management.

weightLoss.title: Obesity Management With Type 2 Diabetes
weightLoss.description: ATTAIN-2 Phase 3 trial showed 9.6% weight loss (21.2 lbs) with 17.2mg in patients with both obesity and diabetes. 9mg achieved 7% (15.9 lbs), 5.5mg achieved 5.1% (11.7 lbs).

weightLoss.title: Cardiometabolic Risk Factor Improvement
weightLoss.description: Significant improvements in waist circumference, systolic blood pressure (8-12 mmHg reductions), triglycerides (-20-30%), and non-HDL cholesterol across Phase 3 trials. Comprehensive metabolic syndrome reversal.

weightLoss.title: Prediabetes Prevention
weightLoss.description: Among 1,127 ATTAIN-1 participants with prediabetes at baseline, up to 91% achieved near-normal blood sugar levels vs 42% with placebo, demonstrating diabetes prevention potential.

diabetes.title: Early Type 2 Diabetes Management
diabetes.description: ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 trial showed HbA1c reductions of 1.3-1.6% from 8.0% baseline over 40 weeks. 76.2% of patients achieved HbA1c <7%, 66.0% achieved ≤6.5%, positioning orforglipron as potential first-line oral therapy.

diabetes.title: Insulin Resistance Improvement
diabetes.description: Significant improvements in insulin sensitivity indices and fasting glucose reduction observed within 4 weeks of therapy initiation. Glucose-dependent mechanism prevents hypoglycemia risk in non-diabetic patients.

diabetes.title: Beta Cell Function Preservation
diabetes.description: GLP-1R agonism protects pancreatic beta cell function through multiple mechanisms including reduced glucotoxicity, enhanced glucose-responsive insulin secretion, and potential anti-apoptotic effects.

diabetes.title: Long-term Glycemic Durability
diabetes.description: Biased agonism profile favoring G protein over β-arrestin may reduce receptor desensitization, potentially improving sustained glycemic control compared to peptide GLP-1 agonists. Long-term studies ongoing.

cardiovascular.title: Cardiovascular Risk Marker Reduction
cardiovascular.description: Significant improvements in blood pressure (systolic reductions 8-12 mmHg), triglycerides (-20-30%), HDL cholesterol increases, and reductions in small dense LDL particles observed across Phase 3 trials.

cardiovascular.title: Obesity-Related Comorbidity Management
cardiovascular.description: Being investigated for obstructive sleep apnea and hypertension in adults with obesity. GLP-1 class effects suggest potential benefits for cardiovascular event reduction, pending dedicated outcomes trials.

cardiovascular.title: Metabolic Syndrome Reversal
cardiovascular.description: Comprehensive improvements in all five metabolic syndrome criteria (waist circumference, blood pressure, triglycerides, HDL cholesterol, fasting glucose) demonstrated in clinical trials.

limitations.title: Designed for Oral Administration
limitations.description: Orforglipron was engineered specifically for oral delivery. Its small-molecule, non-peptide structure provides high oral bioavailability (79.1%), eliminating the need for injectable formulation.

limitations.title: No Clinical Development for Injectable Route
limitations.description: All Phase 1, 2, and 3 clinical trials evaluated oral administration only. No injectable formulation has been developed or studied. The entire development program focuses on oral convenience.

limitations.title: Competitive Advantage Over Injectables
limitations.description: Orforglipron's key innovation is eliminating injections while achieving efficacy comparable to injectable peptide GLP-1 agonists like semaglutide and tirzepatide. Injectable formulation would negate primary advantage.

limitations.title: Systemic Receptor Distribution Required
limitations.description: GLP-1 receptors mediating metabolic effects are in pancreatic beta cells, GI tract, hypothalamus, and cardiovascular system. These require systemic drug delivery that topical application cannot provide.

limitations.title: Insufficient Systemic Absorption
limitations.description: Even with orforglipron's relatively small molecular size, topical application cannot achieve blood concentrations necessary for GLP-1 receptor activation and therapeutic glucose/weight effects.

limitations.title: No Clinical Development for Topical Route
limitations.description: No topical formulations have been studied or developed. All clinical evidence supports oral administration only for achieving therapeutic systemic exposure.

latestResearch.title: FDA Approves Foundayo (Orforglipron) - First Small-Molecule Oral GLP-1 for Weight Loss
latestResearch.excerpt: FDA approved Foundayo under the National Priority Voucher program on April 1, 2026 - the fastest new molecular entity approval since 2002. Approved for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities. Available in 6 tablet strengths (0.8mg to 17.2mg) with no food or water restrictions. Pricing starts at $25/month with insurance or $149/month self-pay.

latestResearch.title: Orforglipron, an Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist for Obesity Treatment
latestResearch.excerpt: Phase 3 ATTAIN-1 trial published in NEJM demonstrating up to 11.1% weight loss (24.9 lbs) at 72 weeks with the highest dose in adults without diabetes. 59.6% achieved ≥10% weight loss. Established orforglipron as the first successful oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist.

latestResearch.title: Orforglipron, an Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist, in Early Type 2 Diabetes
latestResearch.excerpt: ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 trial showing robust HbA1c reductions of 1.3-1.6% with 76.2% achieving ADA target HbA1c <7%. First oral GLP-1 without food/water restrictions to successfully complete diabetes Phase 3 trial.

latestResearch.title: ATTAIN-2: Orforglipron in Obesity with Type 2 Diabetes
latestResearch.excerpt: Phase 3 trial demonstrating 10.5% weight loss in patients with both obesity and diabetes, with 72.8% achieving clinically meaningful ≥5% weight loss. Confirms efficacy across metabolic disease spectrum.

latestResearch.title: Orforglipron: Pioneering an Oral GLP-1 Receptor Agonist in Metabolic Disease
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review of orforglipron's biased agonism mechanism, preferentially activating G protein/cAMP pathways while minimizing β-arrestin recruitment. May reduce receptor desensitization and improve sustained efficacy compared to peptide GLP-1 agonists.

latestResearch.title: Orforglipron (LY3502970): A Novel Non-Peptide Oral GLP-1 Receptor Agonist - Phase 1 Studies
latestResearch.excerpt: Detailed pharmacokinetic analysis showing 29-49 hour half-life, 79.1% oral bioavailability, and no food effect. Phase 1 studies demonstrate safety profile consistent with injectable GLP-1RAs with convenient once-daily dosing.

latestResearch.title: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Orforglipron in Type 2 Diabetes: Phase 2 Dose-Response Study
latestResearch.excerpt: Phase 2 trial comparing orforglipron to injectable dulaglutide demonstrating superior or equivalent HbA1c reductions and weight loss across dose range. Established optimal dosing for Phase 3 development.

safetyNotes: BOXED WARNING: May cause thyroid C-cell tumors including medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC). Contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of MTC or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2)
Start at 0.8mg daily, titrate to 2.5mg after 30 days, then 5.5mg after 60 days - gradual titration minimizes GI side effects
Do not use with other GLP-1 receptor agonist medicines
Monitor for signs of acute pancreatitis (severe, persistent abdominal pain with or without nausea/vomiting) - stop taking Foundayo immediately if suspected
Common side effects: nausea, constipation, diarrhea, vomiting, indigestion, abdominal pain, headache, bloating, fatigue, belching, heartburn, gas, and hair loss
May cause dehydration leading to kidney problems - drink fluids to reduce risk, especially with nausea/vomiting/diarrhea
May affect oral contraceptive efficacy - use alternative birth control for 30 days after starting and after each dose increase
May require adjustment of insulin or sulfonylurea doses to prevent low blood sugar (hypoglycemia)
Not safe during pregnancy (may harm unborn baby) or breastfeeding - pregnancy exposure registry available at 1-800-LillyRx
Gallbladder problems reported - monitor for upper abdominal pain, fever, jaundice, or clay-colored stools
Tell healthcare providers you take Foundayo before any surgery or procedure using anesthesia (aspiration risk)
If doses missed for 7+ consecutive days, contact healthcare provider about how to restart treatment
stopSigns: Severe or persistent abdominal pain radiating to back (potential pancreatitis)
Neck lumps, hoarseness, difficulty swallowing, or neck swelling (thyroid concerns)
Severe nausea/vomiting preventing adequate nutrition or hydration
Signs of severe hypoglycemia if diabetic (confusion, sweating, rapid heartbeat, loss of consciousness)
Persistent diarrhea leading to dehydration (decreased urination, dizziness, dry mouth)
Severe allergic reactions (rash, hives, difficulty breathing, facial/throat swelling)
Suicidal thoughts, severe depression, or significant mood changes
Severe upper right abdominal pain with nausea (potential gallbladder problems)
Unusual fatigue, dark urine, yellowing of skin/eyes (potential liver concerns)
Vision changes or persistent eye pain

oxytocin.subtitle: Neurohypophysial Peptide | Social Bonding & Reproductive Hormone
oxytocin.descriptionEn: Oxytocin is a nine-amino-acid peptide hormone and neuropeptide naturally produced in the hypothalamus and released by the posterior pituitary. Known as the "love hormone" or "bonding hormone," it plays fundamental roles in social bonding, trust, empathy, sexual reproduction, childbirth, and lactation. Synthetic oxytocin (Pitocin/Syntocinon) is FDA-approved for labor induction and postpartum hemorrhage control, while intranasal formulations are being researched for autism spectrum disorders, anxiety, depression, PTSD, chronic pain, and sexual dysfunction.
oxytocin.keyBenefits: FDA-approved for labor induction and postpartum hemorrhage control, precise dosing via IV infusion, immediate onset, well-established safety profile in obstetric settings
oxytocin.mechanism: Binds to oxytocin receptors (OXTR) on uterine smooth muscle cells, triggering calcium influx and myometrial contractions. Also stimulates prostaglandin release to enhance uterine sensitivity.Molecular InformationWeight1,007.19 DaLength9 amino acidsTypeCyclic nonapeptideAmino Acid Sequence:Cys-Tyr-Ile-Gln-Asn-Cys-Pro-Leu-Gly-NH2* Disulfide bridge between Cys1-Cys6 forming cyclic structure, C-termin
oxytocin.protocolTiming: Injectable oxytocin is used in clinical settings for labor and delivery. Onset is immediate (IV) to 3-5 minutes (IM). Effects are short-lived requiring continuous infusion for labor.Peptide InteractionsVasopressin (AVP)Monitor CombinationSelankSynergisticSemaxCompatiblePT-141 (Bremelanotide)Synergis

interactions.name: Vasopressin (AVP)
interactions.description: Structurally similar peptides (differ by only 2 amino acids) with overlapping receptor affinity. Both act on oxytocin and vasopressin receptors with potential for additive effects.

interactions.name: Selank
interactions.description: Complementary anxiolytic effects through different mechanisms. Selank modulates GABA while oxytocin acts on central oxytocin receptors for stress reduction.

interactions.name: Semax
interactions.description: No known interactions. Different mechanisms - Semax for cognitive enhancement, oxytocin for social/emotional support.

interactions.name: PT-141 (Bremelanotide)
interactions.description: Complementary effects on sexual function. PT-141 acts on melanocortin receptors while oxytocin enhances bonding, arousal, and orgasm intensity.

interactions.name: Kisspeptin
interactions.description: Both involved in reproductive function through different pathways. Kisspeptin stimulates GnRH while oxytocin enhances arousal and bonding.

interactions.name: BPC-157
interactions.description: No known direct interactions. Different mechanisms - BPC-157 for tissue repair, oxytocin for social/reproductive functions.

interactions.name: SSRI Antidepressants
interactions.description: SSRIs may affect endogenous oxytocin levels. Combined use may have additive effects on mood but should be monitored by healthcare provider.

interactions.name: Benzodiazepines
interactions.description: Both have anxiolytic effects through different mechanisms. Combined use may cause enhanced sedation or additive effects on blood pressure.

interactions.name: Prostaglandins
interactions.description: Prostaglandins enhance oxytocin sensitivity in uterine tissue. Often used together clinically for labor induction.

interactions.name: Alcohol
interactions.description: Alcohol suppresses oxytocin release and may reduce therapeutic effects. Both can affect blood pressure and judgment.

social.title: Autism Spectrum Disorders
social.description: Extensive research on social cognition improvements, though large trials show mixed results. May benefit specific subpopulations.

social.title: Social Anxiety Disorder
social.description: Studies show decreased amygdala reactivity to social threats and normalized functional connectivity in brain regions involved in social processing.

social.title: Trust and Social Bonding
social.description: Research demonstrates enhanced interpersonal trust, empathy, and in-group bonding following intranasal administration.

mental.title: PTSD Treatment Augmentation
mental.description: Promising results when combined with exposure therapy, showing reduced PTSD and depression symptoms and improved therapeutic alliance.

mental.title: Depression and Anxiety
mental.description: Emerging evidence for anxiolytic and antidepressant effects, particularly in stress-related conditions. Works through cortisol reduction and anti-inflammatory pathways.

mental.title: Schizophrenia Social Deficits
mental.description: Research shows potential for improving controlled social cognition and reducing negative symptoms, though results are mixed.

other.title: Sexual Function Enhancement
other.description: Studies demonstrate improvements in libido, arousal, and orgasm intensity in both men and women through central pathways.

other.title: Chronic Pain Management
other.description: Preclinical evidence for analgesic effects through opioid and cannabinoid receptor interactions, though human studies show mixed results.

other.title: Appetite and Metabolism
other.description: Research shows reduced caloric intake and improved metabolic markers, though 8-week RCT did not show weight loss.

convenience.title: Needle-Free Administration

convenience.title: Consistent Dosing
convenience.description: Pre-dosed troches eliminate measurement variability of other routes

limitations.title: Research Focus on Local Effects

limitations.title: Intranasal Preferred for CNS Effects
limitations.description: For social bonding and psychological effects, intranasal route provides superior brain delivery

latestResearch.title: Oxytocin for Oral Ulcer Healing Through MMP-2/TIMP-2 Pathway
latestResearch.excerpt: Research demonstrates oxytocin accelerates oral ulcer recovery by reducing inflammation and regulating extracellular matrix remodeling, pointing to potential wound healing applications.

latestResearch.title: Social Isolation, Wound Healing, and Oxytocin Administration
latestResearch.excerpt: Study shows oxytocin administration rescues negative impacts of social isolation on wound healing in mice, with treated animals showing faster wound closure and less scar tissue formation.

latestResearch.title: Chronic Oxytocin Stimulates Oxytocinergic System in Autistic Children
latestResearch.excerpt: Long-term intranasal oxytocin treatment increases endogenous oxytocin production in children with autism, suggesting potential for sustained benefits beyond acute dosing.

latestResearch.title: Oxytocin in Stress-Related Neuropsychiatric Disorders
latestResearch.excerpt: Comprehensive review of therapeutic potential for anxiety, depression, and PTSD. Highlights oxytocin's anti-inflammatory, anxiolytic, and antidepressant properties across multiple pathways.

p21.subtitle: CNTF-Derived Neurogenic Peptide | Cognitive Enhancement & NeuroprotectionNasal
p21.descriptionEn: P21 (also known as P021) is a synthetic tetrapeptide derived from the biologically active region of human ciliary neurotrophic factor (CNTF). Featuring an adamantylated glycine modification for enhanced blood-brain barrier penetration and metabolic stability, P21 promotes neurogenesis, enhances synaptic plasticity, and has shown significant cognitive benefits in preclinical models of Alzheimer's disease, traumatic brain injury, and age-related cognitive decline.
p21.keyBenefits: Rapid CNS delivery via olfactory and trigeminal nerve pathways, bypasses hepatic first-pass metabolism, potentially lower doses needed compared to subcutaneous, non-invasive administration. May provide more pronounced acute cognitive effects.
p21.mechanism: Intranasal administration exploits the direct anatomical connection between nasal mucosa and brain via olfactory and trigeminal nerve pathways. This provides rapid CNS delivery while minimizing systemic exposure. P21's lipophilic adamantane modification enhances mucosal absorption and subsequent brain penetration.Molecular InformationWeight578.3 DaLength5 amino acids (with modifications)TypeModifi
p21.protocolTiming: Intranasal P21 provides faster onset than subcutaneous administration. Some researchers prefer this route for acute cognitive demands. The top peptide chemist at Ceretropic insisted on intranasal over subcutaneous for noticeable cognitive enhancement.Peptide InteractionsAdamaxSynergisticSemaxCompati

cognitive.title: Acute Cognitive Enhancement
cognitive.description: Intranasal route may provide faster onset of cognitive effects compared to subcutaneous administration due to direct CNS delivery.

cognitive.title: Focus and Mental Clarity
cognitive.description: Users report enhanced focus, mental clarity, and sustained attention with intranasal P21 administration.

cognitive.title: Memory Support
cognitive.description: Enhanced memory consolidation and recall through direct hippocampal access via nasal route.

cognitive.title: Memory Enhancement
cognitive.description: Preclinical studies demonstrate significant improvements in short-term memory, spatial reference memory, and object recognition tasks in normal adult mice and aged rats.

cognitive.title: Learning Acceleration
cognitive.description: P21 treatment accelerated spatial learning in water maze tasks, with treated animals showing faster acquisition of platform location encoding.

cognitive.title: Neurogenesis Promotion
cognitive.description: Enhanced proliferation and maturation of newborn neurons in the hippocampal dentate gyrus, increasing the pool of functional neurons for memory networks.

neuroprotective.title: Direct CNS Delivery
neuroprotective.description: Intranasal route delivers P21 more directly to brain tissue, potentially enhancing neuroprotective effects in targeted regions.

neuroprotective.title: Neurogenesis Support

neuroprotective.title: Synaptic Protection

neuroprotective.title: Alzheimer's Disease
neuroprotective.description: In 3xTg-AD mice, P21 significantly reduced tau hyperphosphorylation, prevented neurodegeneration, and rescued cognitive deficits when administered long-term.

neuroprotective.title: Traumatic Brain Injury
neuroprotective.description: Enhanced neurogenesis by 80%, reversed dendritic and synaptic density loss, and improved memory recall in controlled cortical impact TBI models.

neuroprotective.title: Age-Related Cognitive Decline
neuroprotective.description: Restored neurogenesis to levels approaching young adults in aged rats (18-24 months), with normalized synaptic protein levels and improved BDNF/TrkB signaling.

mood.title: Stress Resilience

pe-22-28.subtitle: TREK-1 Channel Blocker | Shortened Spadin AnalogNasal
pe-22-28.descriptionEn: PE-22-28, also known as Mini-Spadin, is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from positions 22-28 of the parent peptide Spadin. Spadin itself originates from the propeptide (PE) released during the maturation of sortilin/neurotensin receptor-3 (NTSR3). PE-22-28 is a potent and selective antagonist of TREK-1 (TWIK-Related Potassium Channel-1), a two-pore domain potassium channel implicated in depression, neuroprotection, and pain modulation. With an IC50 of 0.12 nM, PE-22-28 demonstrates approximately 300-500 fold greater affinity for TREK-1 compared to full-length Spadin. Preclinical research show
pe-22-28.keyBenefits: Rapid antidepressant-like effects (4 days in preclinical models), hippocampal neurogenesis, synaptogenesis (PSD-95 increase), CREB activation, enhanced serotonergic neurotransmission, potential neuroprotection, extended duration of action (~23 hours)
pe-22-28.mechanism: PE-22-28 selectively blocks TREK-1 potassium channels with IC50 of 0.12 nM. TREK-1 inhibition increases neuronal excitability in the dorsal raphe nucleus, enhancing serotonin (5-HT) firing rate and neurotransmission. This triggers downstream CREB phosphorylation, BDNF expression, and hippocampal neurogenesis. Unlike SSRIs that take weeks, these effects manifest within 4 days in animal models.Molec
pe-22-28.protocolTiming: PE-22-28 has ~23 hour duration of action in animal studies, supporting once-daily administration. Morning dosing is common but specific timing may not be critical.Peptide InteractionsSSRIs (Fluoxetine, Sertraline)Monitor CombinationSemax / NA-SemaxPotentially SynergisticSelankSynergisticDihexaPotent

interactions.name: Selank
interactions.description: Both have anxiolytic and neuroprotective properties. Selank modulates GABA; PE-22-28 blocks TREK-1 potassium channels. Different mechanisms for anxiety/mood support

interactions.name: Dihexa

interactions.name: Cerebrolysin
interactions.description: Both support neuroplasticity and neuroprotection. Cerebrolysin provides neurotrophic factors; PE-22-28 enhances 5-HT transmission and neurogenesis via TREK-1 blockade

interactions.name: BPC-157

interactions.name: Potassium Channel Modulators

interactions.name: MAOIs
interactions.description: PE-22-28 increases serotonergic neurotransmission. Combining with MAOIs could theoretically increase serotonin syndrome risk. No clinical data - avoid combination

mood.title: Depression Research
mood.description: Primary research focus. Preclinical studies show rapid antidepressant effects in forced swim test, tail suspension test, learned helplessness, and novelty-suppressed feeding models. Acts faster than SSRIs with 4-day efficacy window

mood.title: Neurogenesis Enhancement
mood.description: PE-22-28 nearly doubles hippocampal BrdU-positive (newborn) cells after 4-day treatment. 85% of new cells are neuronal precursors (DCX-positive), indicating genuine neuroplasticity

cognitive.title: Cognitive Function / Memory
cognitive.description: TREK-1 is highly expressed in hippocampus and prefrontal cortex. CREB activation supports memory formation. Research interest in Alzheimer's disease and age-related cognitive decline

anxiety.title: Anxiety Research
anxiety.description: TREK-1 knockout mice show reduced anxiety in elevated plus maze. Spadin and analogs demonstrate anxiolytic properties alongside antidepressant effects in preclinical models

neuroprotection.title: Neuroprotection / Stroke

limitations.title: Peptide Degradation Concerns

limitations.title: No Topical Research

limitations.title: No Direct PE-22-28 Nasal Studies

limitations.title: Formulation Considerations

latestResearch.title: TREK-1 Inhibition and A1 Astrocyte Reactivity in Depression
latestResearch.excerpt: Recent research shows TREK-1 blockade inhibits activation of A1-like reactive astrocytes via NF-κB signaling pathway, identifying a novel cellular mechanism for antidepressant action beyond neuronal effects.

latestResearch.title: PE-22-28 Demonstrates Extended Duration of Action
latestResearch.excerpt: Studies confirm PE-22-28 maintains antidepressant activity for up to 23 hours per dose, compared to 7 hours for parent compound Spadin, making it more suitable for once-daily research protocols.

latestResearch.title: Neurogenesis and Synaptogenesis Within 4 Days
latestResearch.excerpt: PE-22-28 and analogs consistently demonstrate rapid hippocampal neurogenesis (BrdU+ cells nearly double) and synaptogenesis (increased PSD-95) within 4 days - substantially faster than any conventional antidepressant.

latestResearch.title: Human Genetic Studies Support TREK-1 Target
latestResearch.excerpt: Variation in TREK1 gene (KCNK2) associated with depression-resistant phenotype in humans shows potentiated neural responses to rewards, providing translational validation for TREK-1 as therapeutic target.

peg-mgf.subtitle: Pegylated Mechano Growth Factor | IGF-1 Splice Variant
peg-mgf.descriptionEn: PEG-MGF (Pegylated Mechano Growth Factor) is a modified form of the IGF-1 splice variant MGF (IGF-1Ec), with polyethylene glycol attached to extend its half-life from minutes to hours. MGF is naturally expressed in muscle tissue following mechanical stress or injury, where it activates satellite cells to support muscle repair. A landmark 2011 human cell study (cited 74+ times) showed MGF significantly increased satellite cell proliferation at doses as low as 3ng/ml, with effects on proliferative lifespan in young but not elderly donors. While in vitro and animal studies show promise for muscle

Unlike traditional ED medications that work through vascular mechanisms, PT-141 directly influences sexual arousal pathways in the brain, making it effective for both male and female sexual dysfunction.

safetyNotes: Most common side effects are gastrointestinal (nausea 43%, diarrhea 33% at 12mg) - typically mild to moderate and dose-dependent
NEW SIGNAL (Dec 2025): Dysesthesia (abnormal touch sensations) reported in 8.8-20.9% of participants at 9-12mg doses in TRIUMPH-4 trial
Conservative start at 0.5mg weekly minimizes GI side effects (13% vs 73-94% at higher doses) - escalate gradually every 4 weeks
Phase III discontinuation rates: 12.2% (9mg) and 18.2% (12mg) due to adverse events - correlated with baseline BMI
Monitor for signs of pancreatitis (severe abdominal pain radiating to back) - discontinue immediately if suspected
Heart rate increases are common, especially in first 24 weeks - monitor cardiovascular status regularly
Contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome
May cause rapid weight loss - some discontinuations due to perceived excessive weight loss

Signs of gallbladder problems: severe right upper abdominal pain, clay-colored stools
Persistent severe diarrhea leading to dehydration or electrolyte imbalances

selank.subtitle: Synthetic Tuftsin Analog | Anxiolytic & Nootropic PeptideNasal
selank.routeDetail: InjectableAbdomen, thigh, or upper arm subcutaneously
selank.descriptionEn: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide analog of the naturally occurring immune peptide tuftsin, developed by the Russian Academy of Sciences. This anxiolytic and nootropic peptide has been extensively studied for its ability to reduce anxiety, enhance cognitive function, and modulate immune responses without the side effects associated with traditional benzodiazepines. Selank works through complex interactions with GABAergic, serotonergic, and dopaminergic neurotransmitter systems, offering a unique mechanism for stress reduction and cognitive enhancement.
selank.keyBenefits: Rapid onset anxiolytic effects, enhanced BDNF expression, improved stress resilience, cognitive enhancement, immune modulation, no tolerance or withdrawal, neuroprotective effects

semaglutide.subtitle: GLP-1 Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes
semaglutide.routeDetail: InjectableAbdomen, thigh, or upper arm (rotate weekly)
semaglutide.descriptionEn: Semaglutide is a long-acting glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes management (as Ozempic) and chronic weight management (as Wegovy). With over 17,000 participants in clinical trials, it demonstrates significant weight loss potential (average 15-20% body weight reduction) through appetite suppression, slowed gastric emptying, and enhanced glucose control.
semaglutide.keyBenefits: FDA-approved treatment with established efficacy for weight loss (15-20% average) and diabetes management. Convenient once-weekly dosing with proven cardiovascular benefits and comprehensive safety data from extensive clinical trials.
semaglutide.mechanism: Subcutaneous semaglutide mimics native GLP-1, binding to GLP-1 receptors to stimulate glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppress glucagon, slow gastric emptying, and reduce appetite through hypothalamic pathways. The 7-day half-life allows weekly dosing.Molecular InformationWeight4,113.64 DaLength31 amino acidsTypeGLP-1 receptor agonistAmino Acid Sequence:His-Aib-Glu-Gly-Thr-Phe-Thr-Ser-Asp-Val
semaglutide.protocolTiming: Inject on the same day each week at any time. If you miss a dose and it's within 5 days, take it as soon as remembered. safetyNotes: FDA-approved medication with extensive safety data from trials of 17,000+ participants
Start low and titrate slowly to minimize GI side effects
Most common side effects: nausea, diarrhea, vomiting, constipation, stomach pain, headache, fatigue, dizziness, bloating, belching, gas, heartburn, and upset stomach
Hair loss and changes in skin sensations have been reported
Contraindicated in personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2)
May cause thyroid tumors including cancer — tell your provider about neck lumps, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, or shortness of breath
May cause gallbladder problems including gallstones — some may require surgery
Can increase heart rate at rest — tell your provider if you feel your heart racing or pounding for several minutes
Increased risk of low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) when combined with insulin or sulfonylureas
Dehydration from GI side effects may lead to kidney problems — stay well hydrated
Tell all healthcare providers you take Wegovy before any surgery or procedure using anesthesia due to aspiration risk
Stop use 2 months before a planned pregnancy — may harm an unborn baby
Not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding
stopSigns: Severe persistent abdominal pain with or without vomiting (possible pancreatitis)
Gallbladder symptoms: upper abdominal pain, fever, yellowing of skin/eyes, clay-colored stools
Signs of thyroid tumor: neck lump or swelling, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, shortness of breath
Serious allergic reaction: swelling of face/lips/tongue/throat, difficulty breathing, severe rash, fainting, rapid heartbeat
Persistent vomiting or diarrhea causing dehydration (decreased urination, dark urine, dizziness)
Vision changes (possible diabetic retinopathy worsening in type 2 diabetes patients)
Severe hypoglycemia symptoms: confusion, shakiness, blurred vision, slurred speech, loss of consciousness
Signs of kidney problems: decreased urination, swelling in legs/feet

sermorelin.subtitle: GHRH 1-29 Analog | Growth Hormone Releasing HormoneNasal
sermorelin.descriptionEn: Sermorelin acetate is a 29-amino acid synthetic analog of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) originally FDA-approved in 1997 for pediatric growth hormone deficiency. Despite discontinuation in 2008 for manufacturing reasons, it maintains excellent safety profile and stimulates natural growth hormone production while preserving physiological pulsatile patterns.
sermorelin.keyBenefits: FDA-proven efficacy, 6% bioavailability, maintains natural GH pulses, preserves pituitary function
sermorelin.mechanism: Subcutaneous injection provides optimal bioavailability with rapid onset (5-20 minutes) and physiological pulsatile GH stimulation
sermorelin.protocolTiming: Bedtime administration aligns with natural nocturnal GH pulses. Avoid food 2+ hours before injection as carbohydrates blunt GH response. Morning administration less effective.Peptide InteractionsIpamorelinSynergisticCJC-1295SynergisticGHRP-2SynergisticOctreotideAvoid CombinationLanreotideAvoid Combi

interactions.name: Ipamorelin
interactions.description: Excellent combination producing 3-5 fold increases in GH release. GHRH and GHRP work through different pathways with proven synergistic effects in clinical studies.

interactions.name: CJC-1295
interactions.description: Highly effective combination - CJC-1295 provides sustained 6-8 day release while sermorelin offers immediate pulsatile effects. Commonly dosed together in clinical protocols.

interactions.name: GHRP-2
interactions.description: Combined GHRH+GHRP-2 produces 54-fold GH increases compared to 20-fold with GHRH alone. Well-documented synergistic mechanism.

interactions.name: Octreotide
interactions.description: Somatostatin analogs directly block GH release by activating inhibitory somatostatin receptors, completely negating sermorelin therapeutic effects.

interactions.name: Lanreotide
interactions.description: Another somatostatin analog that inhibits GH release through direct receptor antagonism, making combination with sermorelin counterproductive.

interactions.name: Prednisone
interactions.description: High-dose glucocorticoids suppress pituitary GH release and reduce GHRH receptor sensitivity. Requires dose adjustments and IGF-1 monitoring.

interactions.name: Insulin
interactions.description: GH antagonizes insulin action through IGF-1-mediated effects. Monitor blood glucose and consider insulin dose adjustments during sermorelin therapy.

interactions.name: Thyroid Hormones
interactions.description: Essential combination as untreated hypothyroidism prevents sermorelin response. 6.5% of patients develop hypothyroidism requiring hormone replacement.

slu-pp-332.subtitle: Synthetic Pan-ERR Agonist | Exercise Mimetic & Metabolic Modulator
slu-pp-332.routeDetail: InjectableResearch compound - IP injection in animals only
slu-pp-332.descriptionEn: SLU-PP-332 is a groundbreaking synthetic small-molecule compound developed at Saint Louis University that functions as a pan-estrogen-related receptor (ERR) agonist with preferential activity at ERRα. Often called an 'exercise mimetic,' it activates metabolic pathways typically engaged during physical exercise without requiring actual physical activity. By enhancing mitochondrial biogenesis, fatty acid oxidation, and energy expenditure, SLU-PP-332 has demonstrated remarkable effects across multi
slu-pp-332.keyBenefits: Exercise mimetic effects without physical activity, significant weight loss (12% in 28 days), 70% increased endurance, 25% enhanced fatty acid oxidation, improved insulin sensitivity, reduced hepatic steatosis, cardiac protection, reversal of age-related mitochondrial dysfunction, enhanced muscle oxidative capacity
slu-pp-332.mechanism: Binds to and activates estrogen-related receptors (ERRα, ERRβ, ERRγ) which regulate gene expression for energy metabolism. Upregulates PGC-1α (master regulator of mitochondrial biogenesis), activates AMPK pathway (cellular energy switch), increases mitochondrial density up to 1.8-fold, enhances oxidative phosphorylation and ATP production, promotes fatty acid oxidation, induces type IIa oxidative
slu-pp-332.protocolTiming: Animal studies used IP injection twice daily. Human administration protocols DO NOT EXIST. Researchers are developing oral pill formulation for potential future human use pending clinical trials.Peptide InteractionsMetforminMonitor CombinationInsulinMonitor CombinationTirzepatideMonitor CombinationS

metabolicHealth.title: Weight Loss & Body Composition
metabolicHealth.description: Preclinical studies show 12% body weight reduction in 28 days without appetite suppression or increased activity. Fat mass gain <0.5g vs ~5g in controls. Decreases white adipocyte size and shifts metabolism toward lipid utilization.

metabolicHealth.title: Insulin Sensitivity & Glucose Control
metabolicHealth.description: Significantly improved glucose tolerance in obese mice with lower fasting glucose and insulin levels. Enhanced insulin sensitivity comparable to first-line diabetes medications through increased glucose uptake and fatty acid oxidation.

metabolicHealth.title: Energy Expenditure Enhancement
metabolicHealth.description: Increases resting energy expenditure by 25% for fatty acid oxidation. Shifts fuel utilization toward lipids (decreased respiratory exchange ratio within 2 hours) without acting as a stimulant.

metabolicHealth.title: Liver Health & NAFLD
metabolicHealth.description: Reduced hepatic steatosis (fatty liver), decreased hepatic triglycerides, and enhanced hepatic fatty acid oxidation. Potential therapeutic applications for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.

exercisePerformance.title: Endurance Improvements
exercisePerformance.description: 70% increase in running time and 45% increase in running distance in preclinical models. Enhanced skeletal muscle oxidative capacity and improved exercise performance detectable 1 hour after acute dosing.

exercisePerformance.title: Muscle Fiber Remodeling
exercisePerformance.description: Increased type IIa oxidative skeletal muscle fibers with enhanced SDH staining (oxidative phenotype marker). Progressive grip strength improvements observed at days 6 and 13 of treatment.

exercisePerformance.title: Mitochondrial Enhancement
exercisePerformance.description: Up to 1.8-fold increase in mitochondrial density in skeletal muscle. Enhanced mitochondrial ultrastructure, respiratory capacity, and oxidative phosphorylation supporting sustained energy production.

cardiovascular.title: Cardiac Function Improvement
cardiovascular.description: Significantly improved ejection fraction in heart failure models with reduced cardiac fibrosis. Decreased cardiac stress markers (Nppa/Nppb) and improved mitochondrial ultrastructure in cardiac tissue.

cardiovascular.title: Metabolic Cardioprotection
cardiovascular.description: Enhanced fatty acid metabolism in the heart through ERRγ activation. Improved survival in pressure overload-induced heart failure with structural cardiac remodeling benefits.

antiAging.title: Mitochondrial Aging Reversal
antiAging.description: First compound shown to directly reverse age-related mitochondrial dysfunction. 8-week treatment restored mitochondrial function in 21-month-old mice (equivalent to 60-70 human years) with improved maximum respiration capacity.

antiAging.title: Cellular Senescence Reduction
antiAging.description: Reduced cellular senescence markers (p21/Cdkn1a) in aging tissues. Reversal of senescence-associated secretory phenotype suggesting potential longevity benefits.

antiAging.title: Caloric Restriction Mimetic
antiAging.description: Mimics metabolic benefits of caloric restriction without dietary changes. May activate longevity pathways similar to CR interventions for healthspan extension.

kidneyProtection.title: Age-Related Kidney Disease
kidneyProtection.description: Reversed age-related increases in albuminuria and prevented podocyte loss in elderly mice. Reduced kidney injury markers (NGAL, KIM-1) with restored PGC-1α expression in kidney tissue.

kidneyProtection.title: Kidney Inflammation & Fibrosis
kidneyProtection.description: Decreased inflammatory cytokines via cGAS-STING and STAT3 pathway modulation. Anti-fibrotic effects with improved kidney mitochondrial architecture documented by electron microscopy.

antiInflammatory.title: Cytokine Modulation
antiInflammatory.description: Balanced production of pro-inflammatory cytokines including reduced IL-6 and TNF-α. Fine-tuned immune response regulation without immunosuppression.

antiInflammatory.title: Oxidative Stress Protection
antiInflammatory.description: Protected vascular endothelium from oxidative stress. Reduced oxidative damage in aging tissues through enhanced mitochondrial function and cellular energy production.

development.title: Oral Formulation in Development
development.description: Researchers at University of Florida are working to refine the molecular structure to enable oral pill formulation for easier human administration when clinical trials begin.

development.title: No Oral Administration Studies
development.description: All preclinical efficacy and safety studies used intraperitoneal injection. Oral bioavailability, absorption, and effectiveness have not been characterized.

development.title: Future Clinical Development
development.description: Oral formulation is anticipated for potential human clinical trials, but timeline for development and testing is not publicly disclosed.

limitations.title: Systemic Delivery Required
limitations.description: ERR receptors requiring activation are located throughout the body in mitochondria, skeletal muscle, liver, adipose tissue, heart, and kidneys. Topical application cannot achieve therapeutic systemic concentrations.

limitations.title: No Topical Studies Conducted
limitations.description: No research has evaluated topical SLU-PP-332. All efficacy studies used systemic administration (IP injection) to achieve therapeutic tissue concentrations.

latestResearch.title: SLU-PP-332: A Breakthrough Exercise Mimetic for Metabolic Syndrome
latestResearch.excerpt: February 2024 publication in Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics establishing SLU-PP-332 as the first successful exercise mimetic with multi-system metabolic benefits including 12% weight loss and 25% increased fat oxidation without appetite suppression.
latestResearch.source: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics

latestResearch.title: Reversal of Age-Related Mitochondrial Dysfunction Through ERR Agonism
latestResearch.excerpt: December 2023 study demonstrating that 8-week SLU-PP-332 treatment reversed age-related kidney decline in elderly mice (21 months old), restored mitochondrial architecture, and reduced cellular senescence - representing the first compound to directly improve mitochondrial health in aging.

latestResearch.title: Pan-ERR Agonist Activates Exercise Genetic Programs
latestResearch.excerpt: April 2023 publication showing SLU-PP-332 induces acute aerobic exercise genetic programs, increases oxidative muscle fibers by 70%, and enhances endurance by 45% through ERRα-dependent mechanisms. First pharmacological demonstration of exercise mimetic effects.

latestResearch.title: University of Florida Advances SLU-PP-332 Toward Human Trials
latestResearch.excerpt: Research team led by Thomas P. Burris working to refine molecular structure for oral pill formulation and advance compound toward human clinical trials. Favorable preclinical safety profile in multiple animal models supports clinical development.

snap-8.subtitle: Acetyl Octapeptide-3 | Anti-Wrinkle & Expression Line Peptide
snap-8.routeDetail: TopicalForehead, crow's feet, between brows, nasolabial folds
snap-8.descriptionEn: SNAP-8 (Acetyl Octapeptide-3) is a synthetic octapeptide developed as a topical alternative to botulinum toxin for reducing dynamic facial wrinkles. It works by mimicking the N-terminal end of SNAP-25 protein to competitively inhibit SNARE complex formation, thereby reducing acetylcholine release and muscle contractions. Clinical studies demonstrate up to 63% wrinkle depth reduction with consistent topical application.
snap-8.keyBenefits: Non-invasive alternative to Botox, reduces expression lines and dynamic wrinkles by up to 63%, safe for sensitive skin areas including around the eyes, no recovery time required
snap-8.mechanism: SNAP-8 mimics the N-terminal end of SNAP-25 protein and competes for binding sites in the SNARE complex. This destabilization prevents efficient neurotransmitter release at the neuromuscular junction, reducing muscle contractions and preventing formation of expression lines and wrinklesMolecular InformationWeight1,075.16 DaLength8 amino acidsTypeAcetylated octapeptide with C-terminal amidationAmin
snap-8.protocolTiming: Apply SNAP-8 to clean, dry skin before heavier products. Morning application should be followed by sunscreen. Evening application can be layered with retinol on alternate days to minimize irritation.Peptide InteractionsArgireline (Acetyl Hexapeptide-3)SynergisticLeuphasylSynergisticGHK-CuCompatibleH

Not intended for use during pregnancy or breastfeeding (insufficient safety data)

stopSigns: Persistent skin irritation, burning, or redness that doesn't resolve within a few days
Allergic reaction: rash, hives, swelling, or difficulty breathing
Unusual skin discoloration in treated areas
Worsening of skin condition or increased sensitivity after 2+ weeks of use
Eye irritation or vision changes if product gets into eyes

ss-31.subtitle: Mitochondrial-Targeted Peptide | Cardiolipin Protector
ss-31.routeDetail: InjectableSubcutaneous: Abdomen, thigh | IV: As directed by protocol
ss-31.descriptionEn: SS-31, also known as Elamipretide or Bendavia, is a small aromatic-cationic tetrapeptide that selectively targets mitochondria. Unlike traditional antioxidants, SS-31 specifically binds to cardiolipin in the inner mitochondrial membrane, protecting it from oxidative damage and improving cellular energy production. This unique mechanism makes it particularly promising for age-related conditions, heart disease, and mitochondrial disorders.
ss-31.keyBenefits: Directly protects mitochondria, improves cellular energy, reduces oxidative damage, enhances exercise capacity
ss-31.mechanism: Selectively binds to cardiolipin in inner mitochondrial membrane, preventing lipid peroxidation and optimizing electron transport chain function
ss-31.protocolTiming: Morning dosing recommended to align with natural circadian rhythms of mitochondrial function. Pre-workout timing for athletic applications.Peptide InteractionsNAD+SynergisticMOTS-cSynergisticHumaninSynergisticGHK-CuCompatibleBPC-157CompatibleThymosin Beta-4CompatibleHow to ReconstituteCalculatorImpo

survodutide.subtitle: Dual GLP-1/Glucagon Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes
survodutide.routeDetail: InjectableSubcutaneous: abdomen (2+ inches from navel), upper thigh, or upper arm
survodutide.descriptionEn: Survodutide (BI 456906) is an investigational dual glucagon receptor (GCGR) and GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R) agonist designed for once-weekly subcutaneous administration. This 29-amino acid peptide with C18 fatty acid acylation represents a novel approach to metabolic disease treatment by simultaneously targeting both energy intake reduction (via GLP-1R) and energy expenditure increase (via GCGR), demonstrating robust efficacy in clinical trials for obesity and metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH).
survodutide.keyBenefits: Dual mechanism for superior weight loss, once-weekly dosing, demonstrated efficacy in obesity/MASH/T2D, well-established safety profile from Phase 2/3 trials
survodutide.mechanism: Balanced agonism of glucagon receptors (increases energy expenditure, hepatic fat oxidation) and GLP-1 receptors (reduces appetite, slows gastric emptying) with ~6-day half-life enabling weekly dosing
survodutide.protocolTiming: Administer once weekly on the same day. Can be taken any time of day regardless of meals. If you miss a dose and it's within 3 days, take it. If more than 3 days, skip and resume regular schedule.Peptide InteractionsSemaglutide/TirzepatideUse CautionMetforminCompatibleSGLT2 InhibitorsCompatibleSulfo

interactions.name: Semaglutide/Tirzepatide
interactions.description: Both are GLP-1 receptor agonists - combining may lead to excessive GLP-1 activation and increased gastrointestinal side effects. Not recommended without medical supervision.

interactions.name: Metformin
interactions.description: No direct interaction. Can be used together as demonstrated in Phase 2 trials with type 2 diabetes patients on metformin background therapy.

interactions.name: SGLT2 Inhibitors
interactions.description: No known interactions. Phase 3 trials allow concurrent use of SGLT2 inhibitors with appropriate monitoring of glycemic control.

interactions.name: Sulfonylureas
interactions.description: Risk of hypoglycemia when combined. Monitor blood glucose closely and consider sulfonylurea dose reduction if needed.

interactions.name: Insulin
interactions.description: May require insulin dose adjustment due to improved glycemic control. Start with lower insulin doses and titrate based on glucose monitoring.

interactions.name: Oral Contraceptives
interactions.description: Delayed gastric emptying may affect absorption. Take oral contraceptives at least 1 hour before survodutide injection.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: May affect warfarin absorption due to delayed gastric emptying. Monitor INR more frequently when initiating or changing survodutide dose.

interactions.name: Other Glucagon Agonists
interactions.description: Risk of excessive glucagon receptor activation. Do not combine with other glucagon receptor agonists.

tb-500.subtitle: Ac-LKKTETQ | Synthetic Fragment (17-23) of Thymosin Beta-4
tb-500.routeDetail: InjectableBelly, thigh, shoulder (SubQ), or larger muscles (IM)
tb-500.descriptionEn: TB-500 is a synthetic 7-amino acid fragment (Ac-LKKTETQ) corresponding to the active actin-binding region (positions 17-23) of thymosin beta-4. Originally developed for veterinary use in horses, this fragment retains tissue repair and anti-inflammatory properties while being more stable than the full peptide. Banned by WADA for its performance-enhancing effects
tb-500.keyBenefits: Superior systemic tissue repair, accelerated healing of muscles, tendons, ligaments, and organs. Enhanced cell migration and angiogenesis for comprehensive regenerative effects.
tb-500.mechanism: Injectable TB-500 provides systemic distribution for whole-body tissue repair. Acts as the principal actin-sequestering protein, regulating cell migration, promoting angiogenesis, reducing inflammation, and activating stem cell differentiation for accelerated healing.Molecular InformationWeight4,963.44 DaLength43 amino acidsTypeThymosin fragmentAmino Acid Sequence:Ac-Ser-Asp-Lys-Pro-Asp-Met-Ala-Gl
tb-500.protocolTiming: TB-500 timing is flexible and doesn't require empty stomach. For localized injuries, inject as close to the injury site as possible. For systemic effects, standard subcutaneous injection sites work well.Peptide InteractionsBPC-157SynergisticIpamorelinCompatibleCJC-1295SynergisticIGF-1SynergisticGHRP
